{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image=Gamagori city Yatsurugi-jinja Shaden.jpg|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Yatsurugi Shrine | image=[[File:Gamagori city Yatsurugi-jinja Shaden.jpg|250px]]
Worship Hall | location=[[Aichi Prefecture]], [[Gamagori City]], [[Miyacho]] Nanaho 139 | coordinates={{Coord|34|48|59.4|N|137|14|53.0|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Yatsurugi Shrine (Gamagori City)}} | deity=[[Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru no Mikoto]] | type=Former [[Gōsha]] | established=[[Kanji (era)|Kanji]] 3 (1089) | 札所等= | festival=3rd and 4th [[Saturday]] and [[Sunday]] of [[October]] }} [[File:Miyamatsuri.JPG|link=|thumb|220x220px|Underwater [[shinko-shiki]]]]Yatsurugi-jinja (八劔神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] famous for its underwater [[Shinko-shiki]]. It has floating {{Ill|mikoshi|en}} that are carried by people across the water in a parade.{{Cite web|url=https://morethanrelo.com/en/floating-mikoshi-miya-festival/|access-date=2023-04-12|website=morethanrelo.com}} {{出典の明記|date=2020年4月}} {{統合文字|劔}} {{location map|Japan mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|34.8165|137.248056}}|caption=Yatsurugi Shrine|width=256}} '''Yatsurugi Shrine''' (やつるぎ じんじゃ, Yatsurugi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in Miyacho, [[Gamagori City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]. The shrine is classified as a former [[Gōsha]] and is known for hosting the {{ill|Miya Matsuri|ja|三谷祭}}. == History == According to the [[Kuni no Myojin Meisho Chō]], the shrine is listed as "Gojōi no Kami, Yatsurugi Tenjin, located in Hōge District." The shrine is an ancient shrine that holds a deep history of enshrining a spirit originally residing in [[Atsuta Shrine]] in [[Aichi District]], [[Owari Province]]. Initially, Yatsurugi Shrine was the guardian deity of the Miyacho area, worshipped by the locals and revered as a war deity by successive warriors. The shrine is said to have been established in [[1089]] (Kanji 3). It fell into disrepair at one point, but in [[1190]] (Kenkyū 1), on the orders of [[Minamoto no Yoritomo]], [[Adachi Tōkurō Morinaga]] oversaw the reconstruction of the shrine buildings. [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]] visited the shrine to pray for success in battle during his travels to [[Suruga Province]] in [[1548]] (Tenbun 16) and again in [[1582]] (Tenshō 10) on his return to [[Hamamatsu]]. In [[1600]] (Keichō 5), the local lord donated shrine lands. Subsequent lords continued this practice, which continued until the [[Meiji Restoration]]. The records in "Mikawa Futamatsukigami" state that Yatsurugi Daimyōjin Shrine in Miyacho held six koku, six to, four shō, and six gō of land. In [[1885]] (Meiji 17), the shrine was officially designated as a [[Gōsha]]. The shrine grounds once covered an area of four chō and four faces, but were reduced during the [[Sengoku period]]. However, the current precincts still cover over 1800 tsubo, with old pine trees surrounding the shrine buildings, evoking a sense of ancient times. The shrine grounds include several subsidiary shrines: Genta Shrine, Tenman Shrine, Suwa Shrine, Inari Shrine, Mikwa Shrine, Akiba Shrine, Shakuchi Shrine, Suijin Shrine, and Konpira Shrine. == Miya festival == {{See also|Miya festival}} [[File:Miyamatsuri.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Sea procession of the {{ill|Miya Matsuri|ja|三谷祭}}]] The {{ill|Miya Matsuri|ja|三谷祭}} is a famous local event known for its grand procession of splendid floats and traditional performances. The festival, celebrated at both Yatsurugi Shrine and Wakamiya Shrine in Miyacho, is said to have started in [[1696]] (Genroku 9) when the village headman Sazaemon received a divine revelation in a dream to conduct a mikoshi procession. The festival, which involves the entire population of Miyacho, has preserved its ancient rituals to the present day. Highlights include the raising of flags, a grand float parade modeled after the [[Gion festival]] in Kyoto, and various dedication dances from each district. The scale and disciplined organization of the festival make it a unique event. The traditional sea procession of the floats was discontinued in [[1961]] (Shōwa 36) due to land reclamation but was revived in [[1995]] (Heisei 8) to commemorate the 300th anniversary of the festival's inception. == annual Events == * New Year's festival - [[January 1]] * National Foundation festival - [[February 11]] * Prayer festival - 1st [[Sunday]] of [[February]] * festival - 3rd and 4th [[Saturday]] and [[Sunday]] of [[October]] * Shichi-Go-San (Children's festival) - Mid-October to [[November 30]] * Great Purification - [[December 31]] * New Year's Eve festival - December 31 == Subordinate Shrines == === Subsidiary Shrine === * Genta Shrine - deity: [[Otoyo no Mikoto]] === Subordinate Shrines === * Tenman Shrine - deity: [[Sugawara no Michizane]] * Suwa Shrine - deity: [[Takeminakata no Ōkami]] * Inari Shrine - deity: [[Ukanomitama no Ōkami]] * Akiba Shrine - deity: [[Kagutsuchi|Hi no Kagutsuchi no Ōkami]] * Mikwa Shrine - deity: [[Toyouke Ōmikami]] * Shakuchi Shrine - deity: [[Saruhiko Ōkami]] * Konpira Shrine - deity: [[Ōmononushi no Ōkami]] * Suijin Shrine - deity: [[Mizuhanome no Ōkami]] == Precincts == File:Le Temple Shintô Yatsurugi-jinja - Le torii d'entrée.jpg|Stone torii gate at the entrance File:Le Temple Shintô Yatsurugi-jinja - Le temizuya.jpg|Chōzuya (purification fountain) File:Le Temple Shintô Yatsurugi-jinja - Le Temple Shintô Ten'man-sha.jpg|Tenman Shrine (subordinate shrine) File:Le Temple Shintô Yatsurugi-jinja - Le Temple Shintô Suijin-gû.jpg|Suijin Shrine (subordinate shrine) == Access == * About a 10-minute walk from [[Mikawa Mitani Station]] on the [[Tōkaidō Main Line]] ([[Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]]). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Yatsurugi-jinja (Gamagori, Aichi)}} * [http://yaturugijinjya.com/ Yatsurugi Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.38fes.jp/ Miya festival Official Website] * [https://www.city.gamagori.lg.jp/unit/kankoshoko/miyamaturi.html Miya festival] - Official website of Gamagori City * [http://www.gamagori.jp/look/detail_56_1_10_0_0.html Miya festival] - Gamagori Tourism Association * [http://www.miyaspa.com/miyamatsuri/ Miya festival - The Extraordinary festival] - Miya Onsen * [http://www.aichi-kanko.jp/search/detail.asp?id=1543 Miya festival] - Aichi Prefecture Tourism Guide * {{Youtube|kO9SSWMHxTo|The Extraordinary Miya festival - Gamagori City}} - [[This is Mikawa Broadcasting Station]] * {{Facebook|38fes|Miya festival}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Yatsurugi Jinja}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Gamagori City]] [[Category:Buildings in Gamagori City]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1]][[Category:qq 1]][[en:Yatsurugi Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]][[Category:mm]] [[en:Yatsurugi Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1]][[Category:qq 1]][[en:Yatsurugi Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]][[Category:mm]] [[Category:shinto shrines]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yatsurugi Shrine}}[[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1]][[Category:qq 1]][[en:Yatsurugi Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]][[Category:mm]] [[Category:Yatsurugi Shrine]][[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Yatsurugi Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[en:Yatsurugi Shrine]][[en:Yatsurugi Shrine]][[en:Yatsurugi Shrine]][[ja:八劔神社 (蒲郡市)]] [[simple:Yatsurugi Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yatsurugi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image=Gamagori city Yatsurugi-jinja Shaden.jpg|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Yatsurugi Shrine | image=[[File:Gamagori city Yatsurugi-jinja Shaden.jpg|250px]]
Worship Hall | location=[[Aichi Prefecture]], [[Gamagori City]], [[Miyacho]] Nanaho 139 | coordinates={{Coord|34|48|59.4|N|137|14|53.0|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Yatsurugi Shrine (Gamagori City)}} | deity=[[Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru no Mikoto]] | type=Former [[Gōsha]] | established=[[Kanji (era)|Kanji]] 3 (1089) | 札所等= | festival=3rd and 4th [[Saturday]] and [[Sunday]] of [[October]] }} [[File:Miyamatsuri.JPG|link=|thumb|220x220px|Underwater [[shinko-shiki]]]]Yatsurugi-jinja (八劔神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] famous for its underwater [[Shinko-shiki]]. It has floating {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|mikoshi|en}} that are carried by people across the water in a parade.{{Cite web|url=https://morethanrelo.com/en/floating-mikoshi-miya-festival/|access-date=2023-04-12|website=morethanrelo.com}} {{出典の明記|date=2020年4月}} {{統合文字|劔}} {{location map|Japan mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|34.8165|137.248056}}|caption=Yatsurugi Shrine|width=256}} '''Yatsurugi Shrine''' (やつるぎ じんじゃ, Yatsurugi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in Miyacho, [[Gamagori City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]. The shrine is classified as a former [[Gōsha]] and is known for hosting the {{ill|Miya Matsuri|ja|三谷祭}}. == History == According to the [[Kuni no Myojin Meisho Chō]], the shrine is listed as "Gojōi no Kami, Yatsurugi Tenjin, located in Hōge District." The shrine is an ancient shrine that holds a deep history of enshrining a spirit originally residing in [[Atsuta Shrine]] in [[Aichi District]], [[Owari Province]]. Initially, Yatsurugi Shrine was the guardian deity of the Miyacho area, worshipped by the locals and revered as a war deity by successive warriors. The shrine is said to have been established in [[1089]] (Kanji 3). It fell into disrepair at one point, but in [[1190]] (Kenkyū 1), on the orders of [[Minamoto no Yoritomo]], [[Adachi Tōkurō Morinaga]] oversaw the reconstruction of the shrine buildings. [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]] visited the shrine to pray for success in battle during his travels to [[Suruga Province]] in [[1548]] (Tenbun 16) and again in [[1582]] (Tenshō 10) on his return to [[Hamamatsu]]. In [[1600]] (Keichō 5), the local lord donated shrine lands. Subsequent lords continued this practice, which continued until the [[Meiji Restoration]]. The records in "Mikawa Futamatsukigami" state that Yatsurugi Daimyōjin Shrine in Miyacho held six koku, six to, four shō, and six gō of land. In [[1885]] (Meiji 17), the shrine was officially designated as a [[Gōsha]]. The shrine grounds once covered an area of four chō and four faces, but were reduced during the [[Sengoku period]]. However, the current precincts still cover over 1800 tsubo, with old pine trees surrounding the shrine buildings, evoking a sense of ancient times. The shrine grounds include several subsidiary shrines: Genta Shrine, Tenman Shrine, Suwa Shrine, Inari Shrine, Mikwa Shrine, Akiba Shrine, Shakuchi Shrine, Suijin Shrine, and Konpira Shrine. == Miya festival == {{See also|Miya festival}} [[File:Miyamatsuri.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Sea procession of the {{ill|Miya Matsuri|ja|三谷祭}}]] The {{ill|Miya Matsuri|ja|三谷祭}} is a famous local event known for its grand procession of splendid floats and traditional performances. The festival, celebrated at both Yatsurugi Shrine and Wakamiya Shrine in Miyacho, is said to have started in [[1696]] (Genroku 9) when the village headman Sazaemon received a divine revelation in a dream to conduct a mikoshi procession. The festival, which involves the entire population of Miyacho, has preserved its ancient rituals to the present day. Highlights include the raising of flags, a grand float parade modeled after the [[Gion festival]] in Kyoto, and various dedication dances from each district. The scale and disciplined organization of the festival make it a unique event. The traditional sea procession of the floats was discontinued in [[1961]] (Shōwa 36) due to land reclamation but was revived in [[1995]] (Heisei 8) to commemorate the 300th anniversary of the festival's inception. == annual Events == * New Year's festival - [[January 1]] * National Foundation festival - [[February 11]] * Prayer festival - 1st [[Sunday]] of [[February]] * festival - 3rd and 4th [[Saturday]] and [[Sunday]] of [[October]] * Shichi-Go-San (Children's festival) - Mid-October to [[November 30]] * Great Purification - [[December 31]] * New Year's Eve festival - December 31 == Subordinate Shrines == === Subsidiary Shrine === * Genta Shrine - deity: [[Otoyo no Mikoto]] === Subordinate Shrines === * Tenman Shrine - deity: [[Sugawara no Michizane]] * Suwa Shrine - deity: [[Takeminakata no Ōkami]] * Inari Shrine - deity: [[Ukanomitama no Ōkami]] * Akiba Shrine - deity: [[Kagutsuchi|Hi no Kagutsuchi no Ōkami]] * Mikwa Shrine - deity: [[Toyouke Ōmikami]] * Shakuchi Shrine - deity: [[Saruhiko Ōkami]] * Konpira Shrine - deity: [[Ōmononushi no Ōkami]] * Suijin Shrine - deity: [[Mizuhanome no Ōkami]] == Precincts == File:Le Temple Shintô Yatsurugi-jinja - Le torii d'entrée.jpg|Stone torii gate at the entrance File:Le Temple Shintô Yatsurugi-jinja - Le temizuya.jpg|Chōzuya (purification fountain) File:Le Temple Shintô Yatsurugi-jinja - Le Temple Shintô Ten'man-sha.jpg|Tenman Shrine (subordinate shrine) File:Le Temple Shintô Yatsurugi-jinja - Le Temple Shintô Suijin-gû.jpg|Suijin Shrine (subordinate shrine) == Access == * About a 10-minute walk from [[Mikawa Mitani Station]] on the [[Tōkaidō Main Line]] ([[Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]]). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Yatsurugi-jinja (Gamagori, Aichi)}} * [http://yaturugijinjya.com/ Yatsurugi Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.38fes.jp/ Miya festival Official Website] * [https://www.city.gamagori.lg.jp/unit/kankoshoko/miyamaturi.html Miya festival] - Official website of Gamagori City * [http://www.gamagori.jp/look/detail_56_1_10_0_0.html Miya festival] - Gamagori Tourism Association * [http://www.miyaspa.com/miyamatsuri/ Miya festival - The Extraordinary festival] - Miya Onsen * [http://www.aichi-kanko.jp/search/detail.asp?id=1543 Miya festival] - Aichi Prefecture Tourism Guide * {{Youtube|kO9SSWMHxTo|The Extraordinary Miya festival - Gamagori City}} - [[This is Mikawa Broadcasting Station]] * {{Facebook|38fes|Miya festival}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Yatsurugi Jinja}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Gamagori City]] [[Category:Buildings in Gamagori City]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1]][[Category:11 1]][[en:Yatsurugi Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]][[Category:mm]] [[en:Yatsurugi Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1]][[Category:11 1]][[en:Yatsurugi Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]][[Category:mm]] [[Category:shinto shrines]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yatsurugi Shrine}}[[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1]][[Category:11 1]][[en:Yatsurugi Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]][[Category:mm]] [[Category:Yatsurugi Shrine]][[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Yatsurugi Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[en:Yatsurugi Shrine]][[en:Yatsurugi Shrine]][[en:Yatsurugi Shrine]][[ja:八劔神社 (蒲郡市)]] [[simple:Yatsurugi Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Yatsurugi Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]]

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image=Gamagori city Yatsurugi-jinja Shaden.jpg|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Yatsurugi Shrine | image=[[File:Gamagori city Yatsurugi-jinja Shaden.jpg|250px]]
Worship Hall | location=[[Aichi Prefecture]], [[Gamagori City]], [[Miyacho]] Nanaho 139 | coordinates={{Coord|34|48|59.4|N|137|14|53.0|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Yatsurugi Shrine (Gamagori City)}} | deity=[[Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru no Mikoto]] | type=Former [[Gōsha]] | established=[[Kanji (era)|Kanji]] 3 (1089) | 札所等= | festival=3rd and 4th [[Saturday]] and [[Sunday]] of [[October]] }} [[File:Miyamatsuri.JPG|link=|thumb|220x220px|Underwater [[shinko-shiki]]]]Yatsurugi-jinja (八劔神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] famous for its underwater [[Shinko-shiki]]. It has floating {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|mikoshi|en}} that are carried by people across the water in a parade.{{Cite web|url=https://morethanrelo.com/en/floating-mikoshi-miya-festival/|access-date=2023-04-12|website=morethanrelo.com}} {{出典の明記|date=2020年4月}} {{統合文字|劔}} {{location map|Japan mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|34.8165|137.248056}}|caption=Yatsurugi Shrine|width=256}} '''Yatsurugi Shrine''' (やつるぎ じんじゃ, Yatsurugi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in Miyacho, [[Gamagori City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]. The shrine is classified as a former [[Gōsha]] and is known for hosting the {{ill|Miya Matsuri|ja|三谷祭}}. == History == According to the [[Kuni no Myojin Meisho Chō]], the shrine is listed as "Gojōi no Kami, Yatsurugi Tenjin, located in Hōge District." The shrine is an ancient shrine that holds a deep history of enshrining a spirit originally residing in [[Atsuta Shrine]] in [[Aichi District]], [[Owari Province]]. Initially, Yatsurugi Shrine was the guardian deity of the Miyacho area, worshipped by the locals and revered as a war deity by successive warriors. The shrine is said to have been established in [[1089]] (Kanji 3). It fell into disrepair at one point, but in [[1190]] (Kenkyū 1), on the orders of [[Minamoto no Yoritomo]], [[Adachi Tōkurō Morinaga]] oversaw the reconstruction of the shrine buildings. [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]] visited the shrine to pray for success in battle during his travels to [[Suruga Province]] in [[1548]] (Tenbun 16) and again in [[1582]] (Tenshō 10) on his return to [[Hamamatsu]]. In [[1600]] (Keichō 5), the local lord donated shrine lands. Subsequent lords continued this practice, which continued until the [[Meiji Restoration]]. The records in "Mikawa Futamatsukigami" state that Yatsurugi Daimyōjin Shrine in Miyacho held six koku, six to, four shō, and six gō of land. In [[1885]] (Meiji 17), the shrine was officially designated as a [[Gōsha]]. The shrine grounds once covered an area of four chō and four faces, but were reduced during the [[Sengoku period]]. However, the current precincts still cover over 1800 tsubo, with old pine trees surrounding the shrine buildings, evoking a sense of ancient times. The shrine grounds include several subsidiary shrines: Genta Shrine, Tenman Shrine, Suwa Shrine, Inari Shrine, Mikwa Shrine, Akiba Shrine, Shakuchi Shrine, Suijin Shrine, and Konpira Shrine. == Miya festival == {{See also|Miya festival}} [[File:Miyamatsuri.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Sea procession of the {{ill|Miya Matsuri|ja|三谷祭}}]] The {{ill|Miya Matsuri|ja|三谷祭}} is a famous local event known for its grand procession of splendid floats and traditional performances. The festival, celebrated at both Yatsurugi Shrine and Wakamiya Shrine in Miyacho, is said to have started in [[1696]] (Genroku 9) when the village headman Sazaemon received a divine revelation in a dream to conduct a mikoshi procession. The festival, which involves the entire population of Miyacho, has preserved its ancient rituals to the present day. Highlights include the raising of flags, a grand float parade modeled after the [[Gion festival]] in Kyoto, and various dedication dances from each district. The scale and disciplined organization of the festival make it a unique event. The traditional sea procession of the floats was discontinued in [[1961]] (Shōwa 36) due to land reclamation but was revived in [[1995]] (Heisei 8) to commemorate the 300th anniversary of the festival's inception. == annual Events == * New Year's festival - [[January 1]] * National Foundation festival - [[February 11]] * Prayer festival - 1st [[Sunday]] of [[February]] * festival - 3rd and 4th [[Saturday]] and [[Sunday]] of [[October]] * Shichi-Go-San (Children's festival) - Mid-October to [[November 30]] * Great Purification - [[December 31]] * New Year's Eve festival - December 31 == Subordinate Shrines == === Subsidiary Shrine === * Genta Shrine - deity: [[Otoyo no Mikoto]] === Subordinate Shrines === * Tenman Shrine - deity: [[Sugawara no Michizane]] * Suwa Shrine - deity: [[Takeminakata no Ōkami]] * Inari Shrine - deity: [[Ukanomitama no Ōkami]] * Akiba Shrine - deity: [[Kagutsuchi|Hi no Kagutsuchi no Ōkami]] * Mikwa Shrine - deity: [[Toyouke Ōmikami]] * Shakuchi Shrine - deity: [[Saruhiko Ōkami]] * Konpira Shrine - deity: [[Ōmononushi no Ōkami]] * Suijin Shrine - deity: [[Mizuhanome no Ōkami]] == Precincts == File:Le Temple Shintô Yatsurugi-jinja - Le torii d'entrée.jpg|Stone torii gate at the entrance File:Le Temple Shintô Yatsurugi-jinja - Le temizuya.jpg|Chōzuya (purification fountain) File:Le Temple Shintô Yatsurugi-jinja - Le Temple Shintô Ten'man-sha.jpg|Tenman Shrine (subordinate shrine) File:Le Temple Shintô Yatsurugi-jinja - Le Temple Shintô Suijin-gû.jpg|Suijin Shrine (subordinate shrine) == Access == * About a 10-minute walk from [[Mikawa Mitani Station]] on the [[Tōkaidō Main Line]] ([[Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]]). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Yatsurugi-jinja (Gamagori, Aichi)}} * [http://yaturugijinjya.com/ Yatsurugi Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.38fes.jp/ Miya festival Official Website] * [https://www.city.gamagori.lg.jp/unit/kankoshoko/miyamaturi.html Miya festival] - Official website of Gamagori City * [http://www.gamagori.jp/look/detail_56_1_10_0_0.html Miya festival] - Gamagori Tourism Association * [http://www.miyaspa.com/miyamatsuri/ Miya festival - The Extraordinary festival] - Miya Onsen * [http://www.aichi-kanko.jp/search/detail.asp?id=1543 Miya festival] - Aichi Prefecture Tourism Guide * {{Youtube|kO9SSWMHxTo|The Extraordinary Miya festival - Gamagori City}} - [[This is Mikawa Broadcasting Station]] * {{Facebook|38fes|Miya festival}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Yatsurugi Jinja}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Gamagori City]] [[Category:Buildings in Gamagori City]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1]][[en:Yatsurugi Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]][[Category:mm]] [[en:Yatsurugi Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1]][[en:Yatsurugi Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]][[Category:mm]] [[Category:shinto shrines]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yatsurugi Shrine}}[[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1]][[en:Yatsurugi Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]][[Category:mm]] [[Category:Yatsurugi Shrine]][[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Yatsurugi Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[en:Yatsurugi Shrine]][[en:Yatsurugi Shrine]][[en:Yatsurugi Shrine]][[ja:八劔神社 (蒲郡市)]] [[simple:Yatsurugi Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Yatsurugi Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image=Gamagori city Yatsurugi-jinja Shaden.jpg|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Yatsurugi Shrine | image=[[File:Gamagori city Yatsurugi-jinja Shaden.jpg|250px]]
Worship Hall | location=[[Aichi Prefecture]], [[Gamagori City]], [[Miyacho]] Nanaho 139 | coordinates={{Coord|34|48|59.4|N|137|14|53.0|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Yatsurugi Shrine (Gamagori City)}} | deity=[[Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru no Mikoto]] | type=Former [[Gōsha]] | established=[[Kanji (era)|Kanji]] 3 (1089) | 札所等= | festival=3rd and 4th [[Saturday]] and [[Sunday]] of [[October]] }} [[File:Miyamatsuri.JPG|link=|thumb|220x220px|Underwater [[shinko-shiki]]]]Yatsurugi-jinja (八劔神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] famous for its underwater [[Shinko-shiki]]. It has floating {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|mikoshi|en}} that are carried by people across the water in a parade.{{Cite web|url=https://morethanrelo.com/en/floating-mikoshi-miya-festival/|access-date=2023-04-12|website=morethanrelo.com}} {{出典の明記|date=2020th year 4月}} {{統合文字|劔}} {{location map|Japan mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|34.8165|137.248056}}|caption=Yatsurugi Shrine|width=256}} '''Yatsurugi Shrine''' (やつるぎ じんじゃ, Yatsurugi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in Miyacho, [[Gamagori City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]. The shrine is classified as a former [[Gōsha]] and is known for hosting the {{ill|Miya Matsuri|ja|三谷祭}}. == History == According to the [[Kuni no Myojin Meisho Chō]], the shrine is listed as "Gojōi no Kami, Yatsurugi Tenjin, located in Hōge District." The shrine is an ancient shrine that holds a deep history of enshrining a spirit originally residing in [[Atsuta Shrine]] in [[Aichi District]], [[Owari Province]]. Initially, Yatsurugi Shrine was the guardian deity of the Miyacho area, worshipped by the locals and revered as a war deity by successive warriors. The shrine is said to have been established in [[1089]] (Kanji 3). It fell into disrepair at one point, but in [[1190]] (Kenkyū 1), on the orders of [[Minamoto no Yoritomo]], [[Adachi Tōkurō Morinaga]] oversaw the reconstruction of the shrine buildings. [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]] visited the shrine to pray for success in battle during his travels to [[Suruga Province]] in [[1548]] (Tenbun 16) and again in [[1582]] (Tenshō 10) on his return to [[Hamamatsu]]. In [[1600]] (Keichō 5), the local lord donated shrine lands. Subsequent lords continued this practice, which continued until the [[Meiji Restoration]]. The records in "Mikawa Futamatsukigami" state that Yatsurugi Daimyōjin Shrine in Miyacho held six koku, six to, four shō, and six gō of land. In [[1885]] (Meiji 17), the shrine was officially designated as a [[Gōsha]]. The shrine grounds once covered an area of four chō and four faces, but were reduced during the [[Sengoku period]]. However, the current precincts still cover over 1800 tsubo, with old pine trees surrounding the shrine buildings, evoking a sense of ancient times. The shrine grounds include several subsidiary shrines: Genta Shrine, Tenman Shrine, Suwa Shrine, Inari Shrine, Mikwa Shrine, Akiba Shrine, Shakuchi Shrine, Suijin Shrine, and Konpira Shrine. == Miya festival == {{See also|Miya festival}} [[File:Miyamatsuri.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Sea procession of the {{ill|Miya Matsuri|ja|三谷祭}}]] The {{ill|Miya Matsuri|ja|三谷祭}} is a famous local event known for its grand procession of splendid floats and traditional performances. The festival, celebrated at both Yatsurugi Shrine and Wakamiya Shrine in Miyacho, is said to have started in [[1696]] (Genroku 9) when the village headman Sazaemon received a divine revelation in a dream to conduct a mikoshi procession. The festival, which involves the entire population of Miyacho, has preserved its ancient rituals to the present day. Highlights include the raising of flags, a grand float parade modeled after the [[Gion festival]] in Kyoto, and various dedication dances from each district. The scale and disciplined organization of the festival make it a unique event. The traditional sea procession of the floats was discontinued in [[1961]] (Shōwa 36) due to land reclamation but was revived in [[1995]] (Heisei 8) to commemorate the 300th anniversary of the festival's inception. == annual Events == * New Year's festival - [[January 1]] * National Foundation festival - [[February 11]] * Prayer festival - 1st [[Sunday]] of [[February]] * festival - 3rd and 4th [[Saturday]] and [[Sunday]] of [[October]] * Shichi-Go-San (Children's festival) - Mid-October to [[November 30]] * Great Purification - [[December 31]] * New Year's Eve festival - December 31 == Subordinate Shrines == === Subsidiary Shrine === * Genta Shrine - deity: [[Otoyo no Mikoto]] === Subordinate Shrines === * Tenman Shrine - deity: [[Sugawara no Michizane]] * Suwa Shrine - deity: [[Takeminakata no Ōkami]] * Inari Shrine - deity: [[Ukanomitama no Ōkami]] * Akiba Shrine - deity: [[Kagutsuchi|Hi no Kagutsuchi no Ōkami]] * Mikwa Shrine - deity: [[Toyouke Ōmikami]] * Shakuchi Shrine - deity: [[Saruhiko Ōkami]] * Konpira Shrine - deity: [[Ōmononushi no Ōkami]] * Suijin Shrine - deity: [[Mizuhanome no Ōkami]] == Precincts == File:Le Temple Shintô Yatsurugi-jinja - Le torii d'entrée.jpg|Stone torii gate at the entrance File:Le Temple Shintô Yatsurugi-jinja - Le temizuya.jpg|Chōzuya (purification fountain) File:Le Temple Shintô Yatsurugi-jinja - Le Temple Shintô Ten'man-sha.jpg|Tenman Shrine (subordinate shrine) File:Le Temple Shintô Yatsurugi-jinja - Le Temple Shintô Suijin-gû.jpg|Suijin Shrine (subordinate shrine) == Access == * About a 10-minute walk from [[Mikawa Mitani Station]] on the [[Tōkaidō Main Line]] ([[Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]]). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Yatsurugi-jinja (Gamagori, Aichi)}} * [http://yaturugijinjya.com/ Yatsurugi Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.38fes.jp/ Miya festival Official Website] * [https://www.city.gamagori.lg.jp/unit/kankoshoko/miyamaturi.html Miya festival] - Official website of Gamagori City * [http://www.gamagori.jp/look/detail_56_1_10_0_0.html Miya festival] - Gamagori Tourism Association * [http://www.miyaspa.com/miyamatsuri/ Miya festival - The Extraordinary festival] - Miya Onsen * [http://www.aichi-kanko.jp/search/detail.asp?id=1543 Miya festival] - Aichi Prefecture Tourism Guide * {{Youtube|kO9SSWMHxTo|The Extraordinary Miya festival - Gamagori City}} - [[This is Mikawa Broadcasting Station]] * {{Facebook|38fes|Miya festival}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Yatsurugi Jinja}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Gamagori City]] [[Category:Buildings in Gamagori City]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yatsurugi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[en:Yatsurugi Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]][[Category:mm]] [[en:Yatsurugi Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yatsurugi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[en:Yatsurugi Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]][[Category:mm]] [[Category:shinto shrines]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yatsurugi Shrine}}[[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yatsurugi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[en:Yatsurugi Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]][[Category:mm]] [[Category:Yatsurugi Shrine]][[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Yatsurugi Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[en:Yatsurugi Shrine]][[en:Yatsurugi Shrine]][[en:Yatsurugi Shrine]][[ja:八劔神社 (蒲郡市)]] [[simple:Yatsurugi Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Yatsurugi Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]

Miya Onsen * [http://www.aichi-kanko.jp/search/detail.asp?id=1543 Miya festival] - Aichi Prefecture Tourism Guide * {{Youtube|kO9SSWMHxTo|The Extraordinary Miya festival - Gamagori City}} - [[This is Mikawa Broadcasting Station]] * {{Facebook|38fes|Miya festival}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} ]] ]] ]] [[simple:Yatsurugi Shrine]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Gamagori City]] [[Category:Buildings in Gamagori City]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Yatsurugi Shrine]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:11]] }} [[sn:Yatsurugi Shrine]] [[en:Yatsurugi Shrine]] [[ja:八劔神社 (蒲郡市)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yatsurugi Jinja}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Yatsurugi Shrine}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image=Gamagori city Yatsurugi-jinja Shaden.jpg|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Yatsurugi Shrine | image=[[File:Gamagori city Yatsurugi-jinja Shaden.jpg|250px]]
Worship Hall | location=[[Aichi Prefecture]], [[Gamagori City]], [[Miyacho]] Nanaho 139 | coordinates={{Coord|34|48|59.4|N|137|14|53.0|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Yatsurugi Shrine (Gamagori City)}} | deity=[[Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru no Mikoto]] | type=Former [[Gōsha]] | established=[[Kanji (era)|Kanji]] 3 (1089) | 札所等= | festival=3rd and 4th [[Saturday]] and [[Sunday]] of [[October]] }} [[File:Miyamatsuri.JPG|link=|thumb|220x220px|Underwater [[shinko-shiki]]]]Yatsurugi-jinja (八劔神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] famous for its underwater [[Shinko-shiki]]. It has floating {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|mikoshi|en}} that are carried by people across the water in a parade.{{Cite web|url=https://morethanrelo.com/en/floating-mikoshi-miya-festival/|access-date=2023-04-12|website=morethanrelo.com}} {{出典の明記|date=2020th year 4月}} {{統合文字|劔}} {{location map|Japan mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|34.8165|137.248056}}|caption=Yatsurugi Shrine|width=256}} '''Yatsurugi Shrine''' (やつるぎ じんじゃ, Yatsurugi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in Miyacho, [[Gamagori City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]. The shrine is classified as a former [[Gōsha]] and is known for hosting the {{ill|Miya Matsuri|ja|三谷祭|12=draft}}. == History == According to the [[Kuni no Myojin Meisho Chō]], the shrine is listed as "Gojōi no Kami, Yatsurugi Tenjin, located in Hōge District." The shrine is an ancient shrine that holds a deep history of enshrining a spirit originally residing in [[Atsuta Shrine]] in [[Aichi District]], [[Owari Province]]. Initially, Yatsurugi Shrine was the guardian deity of the Miyacho area, worshipped by the locals and revered as a war deity by successive warriors. The shrine is said to have been established in [[1089]] (Kanji 3). It fell into disrepair at one point, but in [[1190]] (Kenkyū 1), on the orders of [[Minamoto no Yoritomo]], [[Adachi Tōkurō Morinaga]] oversaw the reconstruction of the shrine buildings. [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]] visited the shrine to pray for success in battle during his travels to [[Suruga Province]] in [[1548]] (Tenbun 16) and again in [[1582]] (Tenshō 10) on his return to [[Hamamatsu]]. In [[1600]] (Keichō 5), the local lord donated shrine lands. Subsequent lords continued this practice, which continued until the [[Meiji Restoration]]. The records in "Mikawa Futamatsukigami" state that Yatsurugi Daimyōjin Shrine in Miyacho held six koku, six to, four shō, and six gō of land. In [[1885]] (Meiji 17), the shrine was officially designated as a [[Gōsha]]. The shrine grounds once covered an area of four chō and four faces, but were reduced during the [[Sengoku period]]. However, the current precincts still cover over 1800 tsubo, with old pine trees surrounding the shrine buildings, evoking a sense of ancient times. The shrine grounds include several subsidiary shrines: Genta Shrine, Tenman Shrine, Suwa Shrine, Inari Shrine, Mikwa Shrine, Akiba Shrine, Shakuchi Shrine, Suijin Shrine, and Konpira Shrine. == Miya festival == {{See also|Miya festival}} [[File:Miyamatsuri.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Sea procession of the {{ill|Miya Matsuri|ja|三谷祭|12=draft}}]] The {{ill|Miya Matsuri|ja|三谷祭|12=draft}} is a famous local event known for its grand procession of splendid floats and traditional performances. The festival, celebrated at both Yatsurugi Shrine and Wakamiya Shrine in Miyacho, is said to have started in [[1696]] (Genroku 9) when the village headman Sazaemon received a divine revelation in a dream to conduct a mikoshi procession. The festival, which involves the entire population of Miyacho, has preserved its ancient rituals to the present day. Highlights include the raising of flags, a grand float parade modeled after the [[Gion festival]] in Kyoto, and various dedication dances from each district. The scale and disciplined organization of the festival make it a unique event. The traditional sea procession of the floats was discontinued in [[1961]] (Shōwa 36) due to land reclamation but was revived in [[1995]] (Heisei 8) to commemorate the 300th anniversary of the festival's inception. == annual Events == * New Year's festival - [[January 1]] * National Foundation festival - [[February 11]] * Prayer festival - 1st [[Sunday]] of [[February]] * festival - 3rd and 4th [[Saturday]] and [[Sunday]] of [[October]] * Shichi-Go-San (Children's festival) - Mid-October to [[November 30]] * Great Purification - [[December 31]] * New Year's Eve festival - December 31 == Subordinate Shrines == === Subsidiary Shrine === * Genta Shrine - deity: [[Otoyo no Mikoto]] === Subordinate Shrines === * Tenman Shrine - deity: [[Sugawara no Michizane]] * Suwa Shrine - deity: [[Takeminakata no Ōkami]] * Inari Shrine - deity: [[Ukanomitama no Ōkami]] * Akiba Shrine - deity: [[Kagutsuchi|Hi no Kagutsuchi no Ōkami]] * Mikwa Shrine - deity: [[Toyouke Ōmikami]] * Shakuchi Shrine - deity: [[Saruhiko Ōkami]] * Konpira Shrine - deity: [[Ōmononushi no Ōkami]] * Suijin Shrine - deity: [[Mizuhanome no Ōkami]] == Precincts == File:Le Temple Shintô Yatsurugi-jinja - Le torii d'entrée.jpg|Stone torii gate at the entrance File:Le Temple Shintô Yatsurugi-jinja - Le temizuya.jpg|Chōzuya (purification fountain) File:Le Temple Shintô Yatsurugi-jinja - Le Temple Shintô Ten'man-sha.jpg|Tenman Shrine (subordinate shrine) File:Le Temple Shintô Yatsurugi-jinja - Le Temple Shintô Suijin-gû.jpg|Suijin Shrine (subordinate shrine) == Access == * About a 10-minute walk from [[Mikawa Mitani Station]] on the [[Tōkaidō Main Line]] ([[Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]]). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Yatsurugi-jinja (Gamagori, Aichi)}} * [http://yaturugijinjya.com/ Yatsurugi Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.38fes.jp/ Miya festival Official Website] * [https://www.city.gamagori.lg.jp/unit/kankoshoko/miyamaturi.html Miya festival] - Official website of Gamagori City * [http://www.gamagori.jp/look/detail_56_1_10_0_0.html Miya festival] - Gamagori Tourism Association * [http://www.miyaspa.com/miyamatsuri/ Miya festival - The Extraordinary festival] - Miya Onsen * [http://www.aichi-kanko.jp/search/detail.asp?id=1543 Miya festival] - Aichi Prefecture Tourism Guide * {{Youtube|kO9SSWMHxTo|The Extraordinary Miya festival - Gamagori City}} - [[This is Mikawa Broadcasting Station]] * {{Facebook|38fes|Miya festival}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} ]] ]] ]] [[simple:Yatsurugi Shrine]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Gamagori City]] [[Category:Buildings in Gamagori City]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Yatsurugi Shrine]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:11]] }} [[sn:Yatsurugi Shrine]] [[en:Yatsurugi Shrine]] [[ja:八劔神社 (蒲郡市)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yatsurugi Jinja}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Yatsurugi Shrine}}

Worship Hall | location=[[Aichi Prefecture]], [[Gamagori City]], [[Miyacho]] Nanaho 139 | coordinates={{Coord|34|48|59.4|N|137|14|53.0|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Yatsurugi Shrine (Gamagori City)}} | deity=[[Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru no Mikoto]] | type=Former [[Gōsha]] | established=[[Kanji (era)|Kanji]] 3 (1089) | 札所等= | festival=3rd and 4th [[Saturday]] and [[Sunday]] of [[October]] }} [[File:Miyamatsuri.JPG|link=|thumb|220x220px|Underwater [[shinko-shiki]]]]Yatsurugi-jinja (八劔神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] famous for its underwater [[Shinko-shiki]]. It has floating {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|mikoshi|en}} that are carried by people across the water in a parade.{{Cite web|url=https://morethanrelo.com/en/floating-mikoshi-miya-festival/|access-date=2023-04-12|website=morethanrelo.com}} {{出典の明記|date=2020th year 4月}} {{統合文字|劔}} {{location map|Japan mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|34.8165|137.248056}}|caption=Yatsurugi Shrine|width=256}} '''Yatsurugi Shrine''' (やつるぎ じんじゃ, Yatsurugi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in Miyacho, [[Gamagori City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]. The shrine is classified as a former [[Gōsha]] and is known for hosting the {{ill|Miya Matsuri|ja|三谷祭|12=draft}}. == History == According to the [[Kuni no Myojin Meisho Chō]], the shrine is listed as "Gojōi no Kami, Yatsurugi Tenjin, located in Hōge District." The shrine is an ancient shrine that holds a deep history of enshrining a spirit originally residing in [[Atsuta Shrine]] in [[Aichi District]], [[Owari Province]]. Initially, Yatsurugi Shrine was the guardian deity of the Miyacho area, worshipped by the locals and revered as a war deity by successive warriors. The shrine is said to have been established in [[1089]] (Kanji 3). It fell into disrepair at one point, but in [[1190]] (Kenkyū 1), on the orders of [[Minamoto no Yoritomo]], [[Adachi Tōkurō Morinaga]] oversaw the reconstruction of the shrine buildings. [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]] visited the shrine to pray for success in battle during his travels to [[Suruga Province]] in [[1548]] (Tenbun 16) and again in [[1582]] (Tenshō 10) on his return to [[Hamamatsu]]. In [[1600]] (Keichō 5), the local lord donated shrine lands. Subsequent lords continued this practice, which continued until the [[Meiji Restoration]]. The records in "Mikawa Futamatsukigami" state that Yatsurugi Daimyōjin Shrine in Miyacho held six koku, six to, four shō, and six gō of land. In [[1885]] (Meiji 17), the shrine was officially designated as a [[Gōsha]]. The shrine grounds once covered an area of four chō and four faces, but were reduced during the [[Sengoku period]]. However, the current precincts still cover over 1800 tsubo, with old pine trees surrounding the shrine buildings, evoking a sense of ancient times. The shrine grounds include several subsidiary shrines: Genta Shrine, Tenman Shrine, Suwa Shrine, Inari Shrine, Mikwa Shrine, Akiba Shrine, Shakuchi Shrine, Suijin Shrine, and Konpira Shrine. == Miya festival == {{See also|Miya festival}} [[File:Miyamatsuri.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Sea procession of the {{ill|Miya Matsuri|ja|三谷祭|12=draft}}]] The {{ill|Miya Matsuri|ja|三谷祭|12=draft}} is a famous local event known for its grand procession of splendid floats and traditional performances. The festival, celebrated at both Yatsurugi Shrine and Wakamiya Shrine in Miyacho, is said to have started in [[1696]] (Genroku 9) when the village headman Sazaemon received a divine revelation in a dream to conduct a mikoshi procession. The festival, which involves the entire population of Miyacho, has preserved its ancient rituals to the present day. Highlights include the raising of flags, a grand float parade modeled after the [[Gion festival]] in Kyoto, and various dedication dances from each district. The scale and disciplined organization of the festival make it a unique event. The traditional sea procession of the floats was discontinued in [[1961]] (Shōwa 36) due to land reclamation but was revived in [[1995]] (Heisei 8) to commemorate the 300th anniversary of the festival's inception. == annual Events == * New Year's festival - [[January 1]] * National Foundation festival - [[February 11]] * Prayer festival - 1st [[Sunday]] of [[February]] * festival - 3rd and 4th [[Saturday]] and [[Sunday]] of [[October]] * Shichi-Go-San (Children's festival) - Mid-October to [[November 30]] * Great Purification - [[December 31]] * New Year's Eve festival - December 31 == Subordinate Shrines == === Subsidiary Shrine === * Genta Shrine - deity: [[Otoyo no Mikoto]] === Subordinate Shrines === * Tenman Shrine - deity: [[Sugawara no Michizane]] * Suwa Shrine - deity: [[Takeminakata no Ōkami]] * Inari Shrine - deity: [[Ukanomitama no Ōkami]] * Akiba Shrine - deity: [[Kagutsuchi|Hi no Kagutsuchi no Ōkami]] * Mikwa Shrine - deity: [[Toyouke Ōmikami]] * Shakuchi Shrine - deity: [[Saruhiko Ōkami]] * Konpira Shrine - deity: [[Ōmononushi no Ōkami]] * Suijin Shrine - deity: [[Mizuhanome no Ōkami]] == Precincts == File:Le Temple Shintô Yatsurugi-jinja - Le torii d'entrée.jpg|Stone torii gate at the entrance File:Le Temple Shintô Yatsurugi-jinja - Le temizuya.jpg|Chōzuya (purification fountain) File:Le Temple Shintô Yatsurugi-jinja - Le Temple Shintô Ten'man-sha.jpg|Tenman Shrine (subordinate shrine) File:Le Temple Shintô Yatsurugi-jinja - Le Temple Shintô Suijin-gû.jpg|Suijin Shrine (subordinate shrine) == Access == * About a 10-minute walk from [[Mikawa Mitani Station]] on the [[Tōkaidō Main Line]] ([[Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]]). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Yatsurugi-jinja (Gamagori, Aichi)}} * [http://yaturugijinjya.com/ Yatsurugi Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.38fes.jp/ Miya festival Official Website] * [https://www.city.gamagori.lg.jp/unit/kankoshoko/miyamaturi.html Miya festival] - Official website of Gamagori City * [http://www.gamagori.jp/look/detail_56_1_10_0_0.html Miya festival] - Gamagori Tourism Association * [http://www.miyaspa.com/miyamatsuri/ Miya festival - The Extraordinary festival] - Miya Onsen * [http://www.aichi-kanko.jp/search/detail.asp?id=1543 Miya festival] - Aichi Prefecture Tourism Guide * {{Youtube|kO9SSWMHxTo|The Extraordinary Miya festival - Gamagori City}} - [[This is Mikawa Broadcasting Station]] * {{Facebook|38fes|Miya festival}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} ]]]] [[simple:Yatsurugi Shrine]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Gamagori City]] [[Category:Buildings in Gamagori City]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Yatsurugi Shrine]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:11]] }} [[sn:Yatsurugi Shrine]] [[en:Yatsurugi Shrine]] [[ja:八劔神社 (蒲郡市)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yatsurugi Jinja}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Yatsurugi Shrine}}

[[Tokugawa Ieyasu]] visited the shrine to pray for success in battle during his travels to [[Suruga Province]] in [[1548]] (Tenbun 16) and again in [[1582]] (Tenshō 10) on his return to [[Hamamatsu]]. In [[1600]] (Keichō 5), the local lord donated shrine lands. Subsequent lords continued this practice, which continued until the [[Meiji Restoration]]. The records in "Mikawa Futamatsukigami" state that Yatsurugi Daimyōjin Shrine in Miyacho held six koku, six to, four shō, and six gō of land. In [[1885]] (Meiji 17), the shrine was officially designated as a [[Gōsha]]. The shrine grounds once covered an area of four chō and four faces, but were reduced during the [[Sengoku period]]. However, the current precincts still cover over 1800 tsubo, with old pine trees surrounding the shrine buildings, evoking a sense of ancient times. The shrine grounds include several subsidiary shrines: Genta Shrine, Tenman Shrine, Suwa Shrine, Inari Shrine, Mikwa Shrine, Akiba Shrine, Shakuchi Shrine, Suijin Shrine, and Konpira Shrine. == Miya festival == {{See also|Miya festival}} [[File:Miyamatsuri.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Sea procession of the {{ill|Miya Matsuri|ja|三谷祭|12=draft}}]] The {{ill|Miya Matsuri|ja|三谷祭|12=draft}} is a famous local event known for its grand procession of splendid floats and traditional performances. The festival, celebrated at both Yatsurugi Shrine and Wakamiya Shrine in Miyacho, is said to have started in [[1696]] (Genroku 9) when the village headman Sazaemon received a divine revelation in a dream to conduct a mikoshi procession. The festival, which involves the entire population of Miyacho, has preserved its ancient rituals to the present day. Highlights include the raising of flags, a grand float parade modeled after the [[Gion festival]] in Kyoto, and various dedication dances from each district. The scale and disciplined organization of the festival make it a unique event. The traditional sea procession of the floats was discontinued in [[1961]] (Shōwa 36) due to land reclamation but was revived in [[1995]] (Heisei 8) to commemorate the 300th anniversary of the festival's inception. == annual Events == * New Year's festival - [[January 1]] * National Foundation festival - [[February 11]] * Prayer festival - 1st [[Sunday]] of [[February]] * festival - 3rd and 4th [[Saturday]] and [[Sunday]] of [[October]] * Shichi-Go-San (Children's festival) - Mid-October to [[November 30]] * Great Purification - [[December 31]] * New Year's Eve festival - December 31 == Subordinate Shrines == === Subsidiary Shrine === * Genta Shrine - deity: [[Otoyo no Mikoto]] === Subordinate Shrines === * Tenman Shrine - deity: [[Sugawara no Michizane]] * Suwa Shrine - deity: [[Takeminakata no Ōkami]] * Inari Shrine - deity: [[Ukanomitama no Ōkami]] * Akiba Shrine - deity: [[Kagutsuchi|Hi no Kagutsuchi no Ōkami]] * Mikwa Shrine - deity: [[Toyouke Ōmikami]] * Shakuchi Shrine - deity: [[Saruhiko Ōkami]] * Konpira Shrine - deity: [[Ōmononushi no Ōkami]] * Suijin Shrine - deity: [[Mizuhanome no Ōkami]] == Precincts == File:Le Temple Shintô Yatsurugi-jinja - {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image=Gamagori city Yatsurugi-jinja Shaden.jpg|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Yatsurugi Shrine | image=[[File:Gamagori city Yatsurugi-jinja Shaden.jpg|250px]]
Worship Hall | location=[[Aichi Prefecture]], [[Gamagori City]], [[Miyacho]] Nanaho 139 | coordinates={{Coord|34|48|59.4|N|137|14|53.0|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Yatsurugi Shrine (Gamagori City)}} | deity=[[Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru no Mikoto]] | type=Former [[Gōsha]] | established=[[Kanji (era)|Kanji]] 3 (1089) | 札所等= | festival=3rd and 4th [[Saturday]] and [[Sunday]] of [[October]] }} [[File:Miyamatsuri.JPG|link=|thumb|220x220px|Underwater [[shinko-shiki]]]]Yatsurugi-jinja (八劔神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] famous for its underwater [[Shinko-shiki]]. It has floating {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|mikoshi|en}} that are carried by people across the water in a parade.{{Cite web|url=https://morethanrelo.com/en/floating-mikoshi-miya-festival/|access-date=2023-04-12|website=morethanrelo.com}} {{出典の明記|date=2020th year 4月}} {{統合文字|劔}} {{location map|Japan mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|34.8165|137.248056}}|caption=Yatsurugi Shrine|width=256}} '''Yatsurugi Shrine''' (やつるぎ じんじゃ, Yatsurugi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in Miyacho, [[Gamagori City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]. The shrine is classified as a former [[Gōsha]] and is known for hosting the {{ill|Miya Matsuri|ja|三谷祭|12=draft}}. == History == According to the [[Kuni no Myojin Meisho Chō]], the shrine is listed as "Gojōi no Kami, Yatsurugi Tenjin, located in Hōge District." The shrine is an ancient shrine that holds a deep history of enshrining a spirit originally residing in [[Atsuta Shrine]] in [[Aichi District]], [[Owari Province]]. Initially, Yatsurugi Shrine was the guardian deity of the Miyacho area, worshipped by the locals and revered as a war deity by successive warriors. The shrine is said to have been established in [[1089]] (Kanji 3). It fell into disrepair at one point, but in [[1190]] (Kenkyū 1), on the orders of [[Minamoto no Yoritomo]], [[Adachi Tōkurō Morinaga]] oversaw the reconstruction of the shrine buildings. [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]] visited the shrine to pray for success in battle during his travels to [[Suruga Province]] in [[1548]] (Tenbun 16) and again in [[1582]] (Tenshō 10) on his return to [[Hamamatsu]]. In [[1600]] (Keichō 5), the local lord donated shrine lands. Subsequent lords continued this practice, which continued until the [[Meiji Restoration]]. The records in "Mikawa Futamatsukigami" state that Yatsurugi Daimyōjin Shrine in Miyacho held six koku, six to, four shō, and six gō of land. In [[1885]] (Meiji 17), the shrine was officially designated as a [[Gōsha]]. The shrine grounds once covered an area of four chō and four faces, but were reduced during the [[Sengoku period]]. However, the current precincts still cover over 1800 tsubo, with old pine trees surrounding the shrine buildings, evoking a sense of ancient times. The shrine grounds include several subsidiary shrines: Genta Shrine, Tenman Shrine, Suwa Shrine, Inari Shrine, Mikwa Shrine, Akiba Shrine, Shakuchi Shrine, Suijin Shrine, and Konpira Shrine. == Miya festival == {{See also|Miya festival}} [[File:Miyamatsuri.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Sea procession of the {{ill|Miya Matsuri|ja|三谷祭|12=draft}}]] The {{ill|Miya Matsuri|ja|三谷祭|12=draft}} is a famous local event known for its grand procession of splendid floats and traditional performances. The festival, celebrated at both Yatsurugi Shrine and Wakamiya Shrine in Miyacho, is said to have started in [[1696]] (Genroku 9) when the village headman Sazaemon received a divine revelation in a dream to conduct a mikoshi procession. The festival, which involves the entire population of Miyacho, has preserved its ancient rituals to the present day. Highlights include the raising of flags, a grand float parade modeled after the [[Gion festival]] in Kyoto, and various dedication dances from each district. The scale and disciplined organization of the festival make it a unique event. The traditional sea procession of the floats was discontinued in [[1961]] (Shōwa 36) due to land reclamation but was revived in [[1995]] (Heisei 8) to commemorate the 300th anniversary of the festival's inception. == annual Events == * New Year's festival - [[January 1]] * National Foundation festival - [[February 11]] * Prayer festival - 1st [[Sunday]] of [[February]] * festival - 3rd and 4th [[Saturday]] and [[Sunday]] of [[October]] * Shichi-Go-San (Children's festival) - Mid-October to [[November 30]] * Great Purification - [[December 31]] * New Year's Eve festival - December 31 == Subordinate Shrines == === Subsidiary Shrine === * Genta Shrine - deity: [[Otoyo no Mikoto]] === Subordinate Shrines === * Tenman Shrine - deity: [[Sugawara no Michizane]] * Suwa Shrine - deity: [[Takeminakata no Ōkami]] * Inari Shrine - deity: [[Ukanomitama no Ōkami]] * Akiba Shrine - deity: [[Kagutsuchi|Hi no Kagutsuchi no Ōkami]] * Mikwa Shrine - deity: [[Toyouke Ōmikami]] * Shakuchi Shrine - deity: [[Saruhiko Ōkami]] * Konpira Shrine - deity: [[Ōmononushi no Ōkami]] * Suijin Shrine - deity: [[Mizuhanome no Ōkami]] == Precincts == File:Le Temple Shintô Yatsurugi-jinja - Le torii d'entrée.jpg|Stone torii gate at the entrance File:Le Temple Shintô Yatsurugi-jinja - Le temizuya.jpg|Chōzuya (purification fountain) File:Le Temple Shintô Yatsurugi-jinja - Le Temple Shintô Ten'man-sha.jpg|Tenman Shrine (subordinate shrine) File:Le Temple Shintô Yatsurugi-jinja - Le Temple Shintô Suijin-gû.jpg|Suijin Shrine (subordinate shrine) == Access == * About a 10-minute walk from [[Mikawa Mitani Station]] on the [[Tōkaidō Main Line]] ([[Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]]). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Yatsurugi-jinja (Gamagori, Aichi)}} * [http://yaturugijinjya.com/ Yatsurugi Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.38fes.jp/ Miya festival Official Website] * [https://www.city.gamagori.lg.jp/unit/kankoshoko/miyamaturi.html Miya festival] - Official website of Gamagori City * [http://www.gamagori.jp/look/detail_56_1_10_0_0.html Miya festival] - Gamagori Tourism Association * [http://www.miyaspa.com/miyamatsuri/ Miya festival - The Extraordinary festival] - Miya Onsen * [http://www.aichi-kanko.jp/search/detail.asp?id=1543 Miya festival] - Aichi Prefecture Tourism Guide * {{Youtube|kO9SSWMHxTo|The Extraordinary Miya festival - Gamagori City}} - [[This is Mikawa Broadcasting Station]] * {{Facebook|38fes|Miya festival}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} ]]]] [[simple:Yatsurugi Shrine]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Gamagori City]] [[Category:Buildings in Gamagori City]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yatsurugi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yatsurugi Shrine}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Yatsurugi Shrine]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:11]] }} [[sn:Yatsurugi Shrine]] [[en:Yatsurugi Shrine]] [[ja:八劔神社 (蒲郡市)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yatsurugi Jinja}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Yatsurugi Shrine}}

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image=Gamagori city Yatsurugi-jinja Shaden.jpg|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Yatsurugi Shrine | image=[[File:Gamagori city Yatsurugi-jinja Shaden.jpg|250px]]
Worship Hall | location=[[Aichi Prefecture]], [[Gamagori City]], [[Miyacho]] Nanaho 139 | coordinates={{Coord|34|48|59.4|N|137|14|53.0|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Yatsurugi Shrine (Gamagori City)}} | deity=[[Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru no Mikoto]] | type=Former [[Gōsha]] | established=[[Kanji (era)|Kanji]] 3 (1089) | 札所等= | festival=3rd and 4th [[Saturday]] and [[Sunday]] of [[October]] }} [[File:Miyamatsuri.JPG|link=|thumb|220x220px|Underwater [[shinko-shiki]]]]Yatsurugi-jinja (八劔神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] famous for its underwater [[Shinko-shiki]]. It has floating {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|mikoshi|en}} that are carried by people across the water in a parade.{{Cite web|url=https://morethanrelo.com/en/floating-mikoshi-miya-festival/|access-date=2023-04-12|website=morethanrelo.com}} {{出典の明記|date=2020th year 4月}} {{統合文字|劔}} {{location map|Japan mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|34.8165|137.248056}}|caption=Yatsurugi Shrine|width=256}} '''Yatsurugi Shrine''' (やつるぎ じんじゃ, Yatsurugi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in Miyacho, [[Gamagori City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]. The shrine is classified as a former [[Gōsha]] and is known for hosting the {{ill|Miya Matsuri|ja|三谷祭|12=draft|12=draft}}. == History == According to the [[Kuni no Myojin Meisho Chō]], the shrine is listed as "Gojōi no Kami, Yatsurugi Tenjin, located in Hōge District." The shrine is an ancient shrine that holds a deep history of enshrining a spirit originally residing in [[Atsuta Shrine]] in [[Aichi District]], [[Owari Province]]. Initially, Yatsurugi Shrine was the guardian deity of the Miyacho area, worshipped by the locals and revered as a war deity by successive warriors. The shrine is said to have been established in [[1089]] (Kanji 3). It fell into disrepair at one point, but in [[1190]] (Kenkyū 1), on the orders of [[Minamoto no Yoritomo]], [[Adachi Tōkurō Morinaga]] oversaw the reconstruction of the shrine buildings. [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]] visited the shrine to pray for success in battle during his travels to [[Suruga Province]] in [[1548]] (Tenbun 16) and again in [[1582]] (Tenshō 10) on his return to [[Hamamatsu]]. In [[1600]] (Keichō 5), the local lord donated shrine lands. Subsequent lords continued this practice, which continued until the [[Meiji Restoration]]. The records in "Mikawa Futamatsukigami" state that Yatsurugi Daimyōjin Shrine in Miyacho held six koku, six to, four shō, and six gō of land. In [[1885]] (Meiji 17), the shrine was officially designated as a [[Gōsha]]. The shrine grounds once covered an area of four chō and four faces, but were reduced during the [[Sengoku period]]. However, the current precincts still cover over 1800 tsubo, with old pine trees surrounding the shrine buildings, evoking a sense of ancient times. The shrine grounds include several subsidiary shrines: Genta Shrine, Tenman Shrine, Suwa Shrine, Inari Shrine, Mikwa Shrine, Akiba Shrine, Shakuchi Shrine, Suijin Shrine, and Konpira Shrine. == Miya festival == {{See also|Miya festival}} [[File:Miyamatsuri.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Sea procession of the {{ill|Miya Matsuri|ja|三谷祭|12=draft|12=draft}}]] The {{ill|Miya Matsuri|ja|三谷祭|12=draft|12=draft}} is a famous local event known for its grand procession of splendid floats and traditional performances. The festival, celebrated at both Yatsurugi Shrine and Wakamiya Shrine in Miyacho, is said to have started in [[1696]] (Genroku 9) when the village headman Sazaemon received a divine revelation in a dream to conduct a mikoshi procession. The festival, which involves the entire population of Miyacho, has preserved its ancient rituals to the present day. Highlights include the raising of flags, a grand float parade modeled after the [[Gion festival]] in Kyoto, and various dedication dances from each district. The scale and disciplined organization of the festival make it a unique event. The traditional sea procession of the floats was discontinued in [[1961]] (Shōwa 36) due to land reclamation but was revived in [[1995]] (Heisei 8) to commemorate the 300th anniversary of the festival's inception. == annual Events == * New Year's festival - [[January 1]] * National Foundation festival - [[February 11]] * Prayer festival - 1st [[Sunday]] of [[February]] * festival - 3rd and 4th [[Saturday]] and [[Sunday]] of [[October]] * Shichi-Go-San (Children's festival) - Mid-October to [[November 30]] * Great Purification - [[December 31]] * New Year's Eve festival - December 31 == Subordinate Shrines == === Subsidiary Shrine === * Genta Shrine - deity: [[Otoyo no Mikoto]] === Subordinate Shrines === * Tenman Shrine - deity: [[Sugawara no Michizane]] * Suwa Shrine - deity: [[Takeminakata no Ōkami]] * Inari Shrine - deity: [[Ukanomitama no Ōkami]] * Akiba Shrine - deity: [[Kagutsuchi|Hi no Kagutsuchi no Ōkami]] * Mikwa Shrine - deity: [[Toyouke Ōmikami]] * Shakuchi Shrine - deity: [[Saruhiko Ōkami]] * Konpira Shrine - deity: [[Ōmononushi no Ōkami]] * Suijin Shrine - deity: [[Mizuhanome no Ōkami]] == Precincts == File:Le Temple Shintô Yatsurugi-jinja - Le torii d'entrée.jpg|Stone torii gate at the entrance File:Le Temple Shintô Yatsurugi-jinja - Le temizuya.jpg|Chōzuya (purification fountain) File:Le Temple Shintô Yatsurugi-jinja - Le Temple Shintô Ten'man-sha.jpg|Tenman Shrine (subordinate shrine) File:Le Temple Shintô Yatsurugi-jinja - Le Temple Shintô Suijin-gû.jpg|Suijin Shrine (subordinate shrine) == Access == * About a 10-minute walk from [[Mikawa Mitani Station]] on the [[Tōkaidō Main Line]] ([[Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]]). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Yatsurugi-jinja (Gamagori, Aichi)}} * [http://yaturugijinjya.com/ Yatsurugi Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.38fes.jp/ Miya festival Official Website] * [https://www.city.gamagori.lg.jp/unit/kankoshoko/miyamaturi.html Miya festival] - Official website of Gamagori City * [http://www.gamagori.jp/look/detail_56_1_10_0_0.html Miya festival] - Gamagori Tourism Association * [http://www.miyaspa.com/miyamatsuri/ Miya festival - The Extraordinary festival] - Miya Onsen * [http://www.aichi-kanko.jp/search/detail.asp?id=1543 Miya festival] - Aichi Prefecture Tourism Guide * {{Youtube|kO9SSWMHxTo|The Extraordinary Miya festival - Gamagori City}} - [[This is Mikawa Broadcasting Station]] * {{Facebook|38fes|Miya festival}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} ]]]] [[simple:Yatsurugi Shrine]] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Gamagori City]] [[Category:Buildings in Gamagori City]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Yatsurugi Shrine]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yatsurugi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yatsurugi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Yatsurugi Shrine]] [[en:Yatsurugi Shrine]] [[ja:八劔神社 (蒲郡市)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yatsurugi Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Yatsurugi Jinja}}

Miya Onsen * [http://www.aichi-kanko.jp/search/detail.asp?id=1543 Miya festival] - Aichi Prefecture Tourism Guide * {{Youtube|kO9SSWMHxTo|The Extraordinary Miya festival - Gamagori City}} - [[This is Mikawa Broadcasting Station]] * {{Facebook|38fes|Miya festival}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} ]]]] [[simple:Yatsurugi Shrine]] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Gamagori City]] [[Category:Buildings in Gamagori City]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Yatsurugi Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yatsurugi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yatsurugi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Yatsurugi Shrine]] [[en:Yatsurugi Shrine]] [[ja:八劔神社 (蒲郡市)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yatsurugi Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Yatsurugi Jinja}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image=Gamagori city Yatsurugi-jinja Shaden.jpg|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Yatsurugi Shrine | image=[[File:Gamagori city Yatsurugi-jinja Shaden.jpg|250px]]
Worship Hall | location=[[Aichi Prefecture]], [[Gamagori City]], [[Miyacho]] Nanaho 139 | coordinates={{Coord|34|48|59.4|N|137|14|53.0|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Yatsurugi Shrine (Gamagori City)}} | deity=[[Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru no Mikoto]] | type=Former [[Gōsha]] | established=[[Kanji (era)|Kanji]] 3 (1089) | 札所等= | festival=3rd and 4th [[Saturday]] and [[Sunday]] of [[October]] }} [[File:Miyamatsuri.JPG|link=|thumb|220x220px|Underwater [[shinko-shiki]]]]Yatsurugi-jinja (八劔神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] famous for its underwater [[Shinko-shiki]]. It has floating {{ill|mikoshi|en}} that are carried by people across the water in a parade.{{Cite web|url=https://morethanrelo.com/en/floating-mikoshi-miya-festival/|access-date=2023-04-12|website=morethanrelo.com}} {{出典の明記|date=2020th year 4月}} {{統合文字|劔}} {{location map|Japan mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|34.8165|137.248056}}|caption=Yatsurugi Shrine|width=256}} '''Yatsurugi Shrine''' (やつるぎ じんじゃ, Yatsurugi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in Miyacho, [[Gamagori City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]. The shrine is classified as a former [[Gōsha]] and is known for hosting the {{ill|Miya Matsuri|ja|三谷祭|12=draft|12=draft}}. == History == According to the [[Kuni no Myojin Meisho Chō]], the shrine is listed as "Gojōi no Kami, Yatsurugi Tenjin, located in Hōge District." The shrine is an ancient shrine that holds a deep history of enshrining a spirit originally residing in [[Atsuta Shrine]] in [[Aichi District]], [[Owari Province]]. Initially, Yatsurugi Shrine was the guardian deity of the Miyacho area, worshipped by the locals and revered as a war deity by successive warriors. The shrine is said to have been established in [[1089]] (Kanji 3). It fell into disrepair at one point, but in [[1190]] (Kenkyū 1), on the orders of [[Minamoto no Yoritomo]], [[Adachi Tōkurō Morinaga]] oversaw the reconstruction of the shrine buildings. [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]] visited the shrine to pray for success in battle during his travels to [[Suruga Province]] in [[1548]] (Tenbun 16) and again in [[1582]] (Tenshō 10) on his return to [[Hamamatsu]]. In [[1600]] (Keichō 5), the local lord donated shrine lands. Subsequent lords continued this practice, which continued until the [[Meiji Restoration]]. The records in "Mikawa Futamatsukigami" state that Yatsurugi Daimyōjin Shrine in Miyacho held six koku, six to, four shō, and six gō of land. In [[1885]] (Meiji 17), the shrine was officially designated as a [[Gōsha]]. The shrine grounds once covered an area of four chō and four faces, but were reduced during the [[Sengoku period]]. However, the current precincts still cover over 1800 tsubo, with old pine trees surrounding the shrine buildings, evoking a sense of ancient times. The shrine grounds include several subsidiary shrines: Genta Shrine, Tenman Shrine, Suwa Shrine, Inari Shrine, Mikwa Shrine, Akiba Shrine, Shakuchi Shrine, Suijin Shrine, and Konpira Shrine. == Miya festival == {{See also|Miya festival}} [[File:Miyamatsuri.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Sea procession of the {{ill|Miya Matsuri|ja|三谷祭|12=draft|12=draft}}]] The {{ill|Miya Matsuri|ja|三谷祭|12=draft|12=draft}} is a famous local event known for its grand procession of splendid floats and traditional performances. The festival, celebrated at both Yatsurugi Shrine and Wakamiya Shrine in Miyacho, is said to have started in [[1696]] (Genroku 9) when the village headman Sazaemon received a divine revelation in a dream to conduct a mikoshi procession. The festival, which involves the entire population of Miyacho, has preserved its ancient rituals to the present day. Highlights include the raising of flags, a grand float parade modeled after the [[Gion festival]] in Kyoto, and various dedication dances from each district. The scale and disciplined organization of the festival make it a unique event. The traditional sea procession of the floats was discontinued in [[1961]] (Shōwa 36) due to land reclamation but was revived in [[1995]] (Heisei 8) to commemorate the 300th anniversary of the festival's inception. == annual Events == * New Year's festival - [[January 1]] * National Foundation festival - [[February 11]] * Prayer festival - 1st [[Sunday]] of [[February]] * festival - 3rd and 4th [[Saturday]] and [[Sunday]] of [[October]] * Shichi-Go-San (Children's festival) - Mid-October to [[November 30]] * Great Purification - [[December 31]] * New Year's Eve festival - December 31 == Subordinate Shrines == === Subsidiary Shrine === * Genta Shrine - deity: [[Otoyo no Mikoto]] === Subordinate Shrines === * Tenman Shrine - deity: [[Sugawara no Michizane]] * Suwa Shrine - deity: [[Takeminakata no Ōkami]] * Inari Shrine - deity: [[Ukanomitama no Ōkami]] * Akiba Shrine - deity: [[Kagutsuchi|Hi no Kagutsuchi no Ōkami]] * Mikwa Shrine - deity: [[Toyouke Ōmikami]] * Shakuchi Shrine - deity: [[Saruhiko Ōkami]] * Konpira Shrine - deity: [[Ōmononushi no Ōkami]] * Suijin Shrine - deity: [[Mizuhanome no Ōkami]] == Precincts == File:Le Temple Shintô Yatsurugi-jinja - Le torii d'entrée.jpg|Stone torii gate at the entrance File:Le Temple Shintô Yatsurugi-jinja - Le temizuya.jpg|Chōzuya (purification fountain) File:Le Temple Shintô Yatsurugi-jinja - Le Temple Shintô Ten'man-sha.jpg|Tenman Shrine (subordinate shrine) File:Le Temple Shintô Yatsurugi-jinja - Le Temple Shintô Suijin-gû.jpg|Suijin Shrine (subordinate shrine) == Access == * About a 10-minute walk from [[Mikawa Mitani Station]] on the [[Tōkaidō Main Line]] ([[Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]]). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Yatsurugi-jinja (Gamagori, Aichi)}} * [http://yaturugijinjya.com/ Yatsurugi Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.38fes.jp/ Miya festival Official Website] * [https://www.city.gamagori.lg.jp/unit/kankoshoko/miyamaturi.html Miya festival] - Official website of Gamagori City * [http://www.gamagori.jp/look/detail_56_1_10_0_0.html Miya festival] - Gamagori Tourism Association * [http://www.miyaspa.com/miyamatsuri/ Miya festival - The Extraordinary festival] - Miya Onsen * [http://www.aichi-kanko.jp/search/detail.asp?id=1543 Miya festival] - Aichi Prefecture Tourism Guide * {{Youtube|kO9SSWMHxTo|The Extraordinary Miya festival - Gamagori City}} - [[This is Mikawa Broadcasting Station]] * {{Facebook|38fes|Miya festival}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} ]]]] [[simple:Yatsurugi Shrine]] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Gamagori City]] [[Category:Buildings in Gamagori City]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Yatsurugi Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yatsurugi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yatsurugi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Yatsurugi Shrine]] [[en:Yatsurugi Shrine]] [[ja:八劔神社 (蒲郡市)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yatsurugi Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Yatsurugi Jinja}}

revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2014年12月28日 (日) 09:09 (UTC)}} {{神社 | 名称=八劔神社 | 画像=[[File:Gamagori city Yatsurugi-jinja Shaden.jpg|250px]]
拝殿 | 所在地=[[愛知県]][[蒲郡市]][[三谷町]]七舗139 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|48|59.4|N|137|14|53.0|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=八劔神社 (蒲郡市)}} | 祭神=[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]] | 社格=旧[[郷社]] | 創建=[[寛治]]3年（[[1089年]]） | 札所等= | 例祭=[[10月]]第3及び第4[[土曜日|土]]・[[日曜日]] }} {{統合文字|劔}} {{Location map|Japan Mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|34.8165|137.248056}}|caption=八劔神社|width=256}} '''八劔神社'''（やつるぎ じんじゃ）は、[[愛知県]][[蒲郡市]][[三谷町]]にある[[神社]]（[[八剣神社]]）。旧社格は郷社。[[三谷祭]]を開催することで知られる。 == 由緒 == 当社は[[国内神名帳]]に従五位上八劔天神坐[[宝飯郡]]とある。 古社にして[[尾張国]][[愛知郡 (愛知県)|愛知郡]][[熱田神宮|熱田の宮]]に鎮まりせる[[神霊]]を[[分霊|勧請]]移座せる深き由緒を有する。元々、当社は[[三谷町|三谷]]郷[[産土神|産土の神]]として衆庶の尊崇篤く、武神として代々の[[武将]]からも崇められていた。 [[1089年]]（[[寛治]]3年）の創建と伝えられ、一時期は荒廃していたものを[[1190年]]（[[建久]]元年）[[源頼朝]]の命を受け[[安達盛長|安達藤九郎盛長]]は[[奉行]]となって[[神社建築|社殿]]の造営に従事した。 [[徳川家康]]は[[1548年]]（[[天文 (元号)|天文]]16年）[[駿河国|駿河]]への途次、及び[[1582年]]（[[天正]]10年）[[浜松]]への帰途、特に当社に参拝して武運長久の祈願を込めたりと伝えられる。 [[1600年]]（[[慶長]]5年）[[領主]]より[[寺社領|社領]]の[[寄進]]あり。以後、各領主が代々これを手本にして[[明治維新]]を迎えた。[[三河国二葉松|三河二葉松]]に[[三谷町|三谷村]]八劔大明神社領六石六斗四升六合と記されている。 [[1885年]]（[[明治]]17年）[[8月12日]]社格を附せられて[[郷社]]に列せられる。往昔は広さ四[[町丁#町と丁|町]]四面の[[神域]]を有していたが、[[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国]]の乱世に及び縮小されて、今は森と称する地名に僅かにその名残を留めるに過ぎない然れどもなお1800余[[坪]]の[[境内]]には老松鬱蒼として社殿を囲み、古を偲ぶに値する。 境内神社として源太夫神社・天満社・諏訪社・稲荷神社・御鍬神社・秋葉神社・尺地神社・水神社・金毘羅神社の9社を存する。 == 三谷祭 == {{See also|三谷祭}} [[File:Miyamatsuri.JPG|thumb|250px|right|[[三谷祭]]の海中渡御]] [[東海地方|東海]]の奇祭といわれる[[三谷祭]]は豪華絢爛な[[山車]]を従えた[[神幸祭|神幸]]行列と、郷土色豊かな[[奉納]]芸能で名高い。 三谷町の産土神'''八劔神社'''と'''若宮神社'''の[[例祭]]で坂口千夏（[[2014年]]（[[平成]]26年）10月27日）. “2年ぶりに海中渡御 蒲郡・三谷祭”. [[中日新聞]]（中日新聞社）、その由緒は[[1696年]]（[[元禄]]9年）三谷村[[庄屋]]佐左衛門が夢枕の[[神託]]により[[神輿]][[渡御]]を行ったのが始まりといわれ、[[三谷町]]全住民をあげての祭りとして古式をそのまま現代にまで伝えている。 特に創始当時を今日に伝える[[神事]]、勇壮な幟起こし、[[祇園祭|京都祇園祭]]を模したといわれる壮大な[[山車]]の練り、各区の奉納踊りなどそのスケールの大きさと、厳格な規律に統制された[[祭|祭礼]]諸行事は特異なものがある。 伝統の山車海中渡御は海岸が埋め立てられたことの影響で[[1961年]]（[[昭和]]36年）より姿を消したが、[[1995年]]（[[平成]]8年）に祭創始300年を記念して再度復活した。 == 年間行事 == * 歳旦祭 - [[1月1日]] * 建国祭 - [[2月11日]] * 祈年祭 - [[2月]]第1[[日曜日]] * 例大祭 - [[10月]]第3及び第4[[土曜日|土]]・[[日曜日]] * 七五三 - 10月中旬～[[11月30日]] * 大祓 - [[12月31日]] * 除夜祭 - 12月31日 == 摂末社 == === 摂社 === * 源太夫神社 - 祭神：[[乎止与命]]（おとよのみこと） === 末社 === * 天満社 - 祭神：[[菅原道真]]公（すがわらのみちざねこう） * 諏訪社 - 祭神：[[建御名方神|建御名方大神]]（たけみなかたのおおかみ） * 稲荷社 - 祭神：[[ウカノミタマ|宇迦之御魂大神]]（うかのみたまのおおかみ） * 秋葉社 - 祭神：[[カグツチ|火之迦具土大神]]（ひのかぐつちのおおかみ） * 御鍬社 - 祭神：[[トヨウケビメ|豊受大御神]]（とようけのおおみかみ） * 尺地社 - 祭神：[[サルタヒコ|猿田彦大神]]（さるたひこのおおかみ） * 金毘羅神社 - 祭神：[[大物主|大物主大神]]（おおものぬしのおおかみ） * 水神宮 - 祭神：[[ミヅハノメ|水波能売大神]]（みずはめのおおかみ） == 境内 == ファイル:Le Temple Shintô Yatsurugi-jinja - Le torii d'entrée.jpg|入口石鳥居 ファイル:Le Temple Shintô Yatsurugi-jinja - Le temizuya.jpg|手水舎 ファイル:Le Temple Shintô Yatsurugi-jinja - Le Temple Shintô Ten'man-sha.jpg|天満社（末社） ファイル:Le Temple Shintô Yatsurugi-jinja - Le Temple Shintô Suijin-gû.jpg|水神宮（末社） == アクセス == * [[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[東海道線 (名古屋地区)|東海道本線]] [[三河三谷駅]]から徒歩で約10分。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} == 外部リンク == {{commonscat|Yatsurugi-jinja (Gamagori, Aichi)}} * [http://yaturugijinjya.com/ 八劔神社公式ホームページ] * [http://www.38fes.jp/ 三谷祭公式サイト] * [https://www.city.gamagori.lg.jp/unit/kankoshoko/miyamaturi.html 三谷祭] - 愛知県蒲郡市公式ホームページ * [http://www.gamagori.jp/look/detail_56_1_10_0_0.html 三谷祭] - 蒲郡観光協会 * [http://www.miyaspa.com/miyamatsuri/ 三谷祭 天下の奇祭] - 三谷温泉 * [http://www.aichi-kanko.jp/search/detail.asp?id=1543 三谷祭] - 蒲郡観光ガイド * {{Youtube|kO9SSWMHxTo|天下の奇祭　三谷祭　-蒲郡市-}} - [[こちら三河放送局]] * {{Facebook|38fes|三谷祭}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やつるきしんしや}} [[Category:愛知県の神社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧郷社]] [[Category:蒲郡市の歴史]] [[Category:蒲郡市の建築物]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[八劔神社 (蒲郡市)]] to [[Yatsurugi Shrine]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
