revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image=Gamagori city Yatsurugi-jinja Shaden.jpg|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Yatsurugi Shrine | image=[[File:Gamagori city Yatsurugi-jinja Shaden.jpg|250px]]
Worship Hall | location=[[Aichi Prefecture]], [[Gamagori City]], [[Miyacho]] Nanaho 139 | coordinates={{Coord|34|48|59.4|N|137|14|53.0|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Yatsurugi Shrine (Gamagori City)}} | deity=[[Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru no Mikoto]] | type=Former [[Gōsha]] | established=[[Kanji (era)|Kanji]] 3 (1089) | 札所等= | festival=3rd and 4th [[Saturday]] and [[Sunday]] of [[October]] }} [[File:Miyamatsuri.JPG|link=|thumb|220x220px|Underwater [[shinko-shiki]]]]Yatsurugi-jinja (八劔神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] famous for its underwater [[Shinko-shiki]]. It has floating {{ill|mikoshi|en}} that are carried by people across the water in a parade.{{Cite web|url=https://morethanrelo.com/en/floating-mikoshi-miya-festival/|access-date=2023-04-12|website=morethanrelo.com}} {{出典の明記|date=2020th year 4月}} {{統合文字|劔}} {{location map|Japan mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|34.8165|137.248056}}|caption=Yatsurugi Shrine|width=256}} '''Yatsurugi Shrine''' (やつるぎ じんじゃ, Yatsurugi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in Miyacho, [[Gamagori City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]. The shrine is classified as a former [[Gōsha]] and is known for hosting the {{ill|Miya Matsuri|ja|三谷祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == History == According to the [[Kuni no Myojin Meisho Chō]], the shrine is listed as "Gojōi no Kami, Yatsurugi Tenjin, located in Hōge District." The shrine is an ancient shrine that holds a deep history of enshrining a spirit originally residing in [[Atsuta Shrine]] in [[Aichi District]], [[Owari Province]]. Initially, Yatsurugi Shrine was the guardian deity of the Miyacho area, worshipped by the locals and revered as a war deity by successive warriors. The shrine is said to have been established in [[1089]] (Kanji 3). It fell into disrepair at one point, but in [[1190]] (Kenkyū 1), on the orders of [[Minamoto no Yoritomo]], [[Adachi Tōkurō Morinaga]] oversaw the reconstruction of the shrine buildings. [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]] visited the shrine to pray for success in battle during his travels to [[Suruga Province]] in [[1548]] (Tenbun 16) and again in [[1582]] (Tenshō 10) on his return to [[Hamamatsu]]. In [[1600]] (Keichō 5), the local lord donated shrine lands. Subsequent lords continued this practice, which continued until the [[Meiji Restoration]]. The records in "Mikawa Futamatsukigami" state that Yatsurugi Daimyōjin Shrine in Miyacho held six koku, six to, four shō, and six gō of land. In [[1885]] (Meiji 17), the shrine was officially designated as a [[Gōsha]]. The shrine grounds once covered an area of four chō and four faces, but were reduced during the [[Sengoku period]]. However, the current precincts still cover over 1800 tsubo, with old pine trees surrounding the shrine buildings, evoking a sense of ancient times. The shrine grounds include several subsidiary shrines: Genta Shrine, Tenman Shrine, Suwa Shrine, Inari Shrine, Mikwa Shrine, Akiba Shrine, Shakuchi Shrine, Suijin Shrine, and Konpira Shrine. == Miya festival == {{See also|Miya festival}} [[File:Miyamatsuri.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Sea procession of the {{ill|Miya Matsuri|ja|三谷祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}]] The {{ill|Miya Matsuri|ja|三谷祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is a famous local event known for its grand procession of splendid floats and traditional performances. The festival, celebrated at both Yatsurugi Shrine and Wakamiya Shrine in Miyacho, is said to have started in [[1696]] (Genroku 9) when the village headman Sazaemon received a divine revelation in a dream to conduct a mikoshi procession. The festival, which involves the entire population of Miyacho, has preserved its ancient rituals to the present day. Highlights include the raising of flags, a grand float parade modeled after the [[Gion festival]] in Kyoto, and various dedication dances from each district. The scale and disciplined organization of the festival make it a unique event. The traditional sea procession of the floats was discontinued in [[1961]] (Shōwa 36) due to land reclamation but was revived in [[1995]] (Heisei 8) to commemorate the 300th anniversary of the festival's inception. == annual Events == * New Year's festival - [[January 1]] * National Foundation festival - [[February 11]] * Prayer festival - 1st [[Sunday]] of [[February]] * festival - 3rd and 4th [[Saturday]] and [[Sunday]] of [[October]] * Shichi-Go-San (Children's festival) - Mid-October to [[November 30]] * Great Purification - [[December 31]] * New Year's Eve festival - December 31 == Subordinate Shrines == === Subsidiary Shrine === * Genta Shrine - deity: [[Otoyo no Mikoto]] === Subordinate Shrines === * Tenman Shrine - deity: [[Sugawara no Michizane]] * Suwa Shrine - deity: [[Takeminakata no Ōkami]] * Inari Shrine - deity: [[Ukanomitama no Ōkami]] * Akiba Shrine - deity: [[Kagutsuchi|Hi no Kagutsuchi no Ōkami]] * Mikwa Shrine - deity: [[Toyouke Ōmikami]] * Shakuchi Shrine - deity: [[Saruhiko Ōkami]] * Konpira Shrine - deity: [[Ōmononushi no Ōkami]] * Suijin Shrine - deity: [[Mizuhanome no Ōkami]] == Precincts == File:Le Temple Shintô Yatsurugi-jinja - Le torii d'entrée.jpg|Stone torii gate at the entrance File:Le Temple Shintô Yatsurugi-jinja - Le temizuya.jpg|Chōzuya (purification fountain) File:Le Temple Shintô Yatsurugi-jinja - Le Temple Shintô Ten'man-sha.jpg|Tenman Shrine (subordinate shrine) File:Le Temple Shintô Yatsurugi-jinja - {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image=Gamagori city Yatsurugi-jinja Shaden.jpg|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Yatsurugi Shrine | image=[[File:Gamagori city Yatsurugi-jinja Shaden.jpg|250px]]
Worship Hall | location=[[Aichi Prefecture]], [[Gamagori City]], [[Miyacho]] Nanaho 139 | coordinates={{Coord|34|48|59.4|N|137|14|53.0|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Yatsurugi Shrine (Gamagori City)}} | deity=[[Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru no Mikoto]] | type=Former [[Gōsha]] | established=[[Kanji (era)|Kanji]] 3 (1089) | 札所等= | festival=3rd and 4th [[Saturday]] and [[Sunday]] of [[October]] }} [[File:Miyamatsuri.JPG|link=|thumb|220x220px|Underwater [[shinko-shiki]]]]Yatsurugi-jinja (八劔神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] famous for its underwater [[Shinko-shiki]]. It has floating {{ill|mikoshi|en}} that are carried by people across the water in a parade.{{Cite web|url=https://morethanrelo.com/en/floating-mikoshi-miya-festival/|access-date=2023-04-12|website=morethanrelo.com}} {{出典の明記|date=2020th year 4月}} {{統合文字|劔}} {{location map|Japan mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|34.8165|137.248056}}|caption=Yatsurugi Shrine|width=256}} '''Yatsurugi Shrine''' (やつるぎ じんじゃ, Yatsurugi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in Miyacho, [[Gamagori City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]. The shrine is classified as a former [[Gōsha]] and is known for hosting the {{ill|Miya Matsuri|ja|三谷祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == History == According to the [[Kuni no Myojin Meisho Chō]], the shrine is listed as "Gojōi no Kami, Yatsurugi Tenjin, located in Hōge District." The shrine is an ancient shrine that holds a deep history of enshrining a spirit originally residing in [[Atsuta Shrine]] in [[Aichi District]], [[Owari Province]]. Initially, Yatsurugi Shrine was the guardian deity of the Miyacho area, worshipped by the locals and revered as a war deity by successive warriors. The shrine is said to have been established in [[1089]] (Kanji 3). It fell into disrepair at one point, but in [[1190]] (Kenkyū 1), on the orders of [[Minamoto no Yoritomo]], [[Adachi Tōkurō Morinaga]] oversaw the reconstruction of the shrine buildings. [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]] visited the shrine to pray for success in battle during his travels to [[Suruga Province]] in [[1548]] (Tenbun 16) and again in [[1582]] (Tenshō 10) on his return to [[Hamamatsu]]. In [[1600]] (Keichō 5), the local lord donated shrine lands. Subsequent lords continued this practice, which continued until the [[Meiji Restoration]]. The records in "Mikawa Futamatsukigami" state that Yatsurugi Daimyōjin Shrine in Miyacho held six koku, six to, four shō, and six gō of land. In [[1885]] (Meiji 17), the shrine was officially designated as a [[Gōsha]]. The shrine grounds once covered an area of four chō and four faces, but were reduced during the [[Sengoku period]]. However, the current precincts still cover over 1800 tsubo, with old pine trees surrounding the shrine buildings, evoking a sense of ancient times. The shrine grounds include several subsidiary shrines: Genta Shrine, Tenman Shrine, Suwa Shrine, Inari Shrine, Mikwa Shrine, Akiba Shrine, Shakuchi Shrine, Suijin Shrine, and Konpira Shrine. == Miya festival == {{See also|Miya festival}} [[File:Miyamatsuri.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Sea procession of the {{ill|Miya Matsuri|ja|三谷祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}]] The {{ill|Miya Matsuri|ja|三谷祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is a famous local event known for its grand procession of splendid floats and traditional performances. The festival, celebrated at both Yatsurugi Shrine and Wakamiya Shrine in Miyacho, is said to have started in [[1696]] (Genroku 9) when the village headman Sazaemon received a divine revelation in a dream to conduct a mikoshi procession. The festival, which involves the entire population of Miyacho, has preserved its ancient rituals to the present day. Highlights include the raising of flags, a grand float parade modeled after the [[Gion festival]] in Kyoto, and various dedication dances from each district. The scale and disciplined organization of the festival make it a unique event. The traditional sea procession of the floats was discontinued in [[1961]] (Shōwa 36) due to land reclamation but was revived in [[1995]] (Heisei 8) to commemorate the 300th anniversary of the festival's inception. == annual Events == * New Year's festival - [[January 1]] * National Foundation festival - [[February 11]] * Prayer festival - 1st [[Sunday]] of [[February]] * festival - 3rd and 4th [[Saturday]] and [[Sunday]] of [[October]] * Shichi-Go-San (Children's festival) - Mid-October to [[November 30]] * Great Purification - [[December 31]] * New Year's Eve festival - December 31 == Subordinate Shrines == === Subsidiary Shrine === * Genta Shrine - deity: [[Otoyo no Mikoto]] === Subordinate Shrines === * Tenman Shrine - deity: [[Sugawara no Michizane]] * Suwa Shrine - deity: [[Takeminakata no Ōkami]] * Inari Shrine - deity: [[Ukanomitama no Ōkami]] * Akiba Shrine - deity: [[Kagutsuchi|Hi no Kagutsuchi no Ōkami]] * Mikwa Shrine - deity: [[Toyouke Ōmikami]] * Shakuchi Shrine - deity: [[Saruhiko Ōkami]] * Konpira Shrine - deity: [[Ōmononushi no Ōkami]] * Suijin Shrine - deity: [[Mizuhanome no Ōkami]] == Precincts == File:Le Temple Shintô Yatsurugi-jinja - Le torii d'entrée.jpg|Stone torii gate at the entrance File:Le Temple Shintô Yatsurugi-jinja - Le temizuya.jpg|Chōzuya (purification fountain) File:Le Temple Shintô Yatsurugi-jinja - Le Temple Shintô Ten'man-sha.jpg|Tenman Shrine (subordinate shrine) File:Le Temple Shintô Yatsurugi-jinja - Le Temple Shintô Suijin-gû.jpg|Suijin Shrine (subordinate shrine) == Access == * About a 10-minute walk from [[Mikawa Mitani Station]] on the [[Tōkaidō Main Line]] ([[Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]]). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Yatsurugi-jinja (Gamagori, Aichi)}} * [http://yaturugijinjya.com/ Yatsurugi Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.38fes.jp/ Miya festival Official Website] * [https://www.city.gamagori.lg.jp/unit/kankoshoko/miyamaturi.html Miya festival] - Official website of Gamagori City * [http://www.gamagori.jp/look/detail_56_1_10_0_0.html Miya festival] - Gamagori Tourism Association * [http://www.miyaspa.com/miyamatsuri/ Miya festival - The Extraordinary festival] - Miya Onsen * [http://www.aichi-kanko.jp/search/detail.asp?id=1543 Miya festival] - Aichi Prefecture Tourism Guide * {{Youtube|kO9SSWMHxTo|The Extraordinary Miya festival - Gamagori City}} - [[This is Mikawa Broadcasting Station]] * {{Facebook|38fes|Miya festival}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} ]]]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yatsurugi Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Yatsurugi Jinja}} {{translated page|ja|八劔神社 (蒲郡市)|version=99684594|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Gamagori City]] [[Category:Buildings in Gamagori City]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Yatsurugi Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yatsurugi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yatsurugi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[simple:Yatsurugi Shrine]] [[sn:Yatsurugi Shrine]] [[en:Yatsurugi Shrine]] [[ja:八劔神社 (蒲郡市)]]

Miya Onsen * [http://www.aichi-kanko.jp/search/detail.asp?id=1543 Miya festival] - Aichi Prefecture Tourism Guide * {{Youtube|kO9SSWMHxTo|The Extraordinary Miya festival - Gamagori City}} - [[This is Mikawa Broadcasting Station]] * {{Facebook|38fes|Miya festival}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} ]]]] {{translated page|ja|八劔神社 (蒲郡市)|version=99684594|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Gamagori City]] [[Category:Buildings in Gamagori City]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Yatsurugi Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[sn:Yatsurugi Shrine]][[sn:Yatsurugi Shrine]]}} [[simple:Yatsurugi Shrine]] [[sn:Yatsurugi Shrine]] [[en:Yatsurugi Shrine]] [[ja:八劔神社 (蒲郡市)]]

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image=Gamagori city Yatsurugi-jinja Shaden.jpg|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Yatsurugi Shrine | image=[[File:Gamagori city Yatsurugi-jinja Shaden.jpg|250px]]
Worship Hall | location=[[Aichi Prefecture]], [[Gamagori City]], [[Miyacho]] Nanaho 139 | coordinates={{Coord|34|48|59.4|N|137|14|53.0|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Yatsurugi Shrine (Gamagori City)}} | deity=[[Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru no Mikoto]] | type=Former [[Gōsha]] | established=[[Kanji (era)|Kanji]] 3 (1089) | 札所等= | festival=3rd and 4th [[Saturday]] and [[Sunday]] of [[October]] }} [[File:Miyamatsuri.JPG|link=|thumb|220x220px|Underwater [[shinko-shiki]]]]Yatsurugi-jinja (八劔神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] famous for its underwater [[Shinko-shiki]]. It has floating {{ill|mikoshi|en}} that are carried by people across the water in a parade.{{Cite web|url=https://morethanrelo.com/en/floating-mikoshi-miya-festival/|access-date=2023-04-12|website=morethanrelo.com}} {{出典の明記|date=2020th year 4月}} {{統合文字|劔}} {{location map|Japan mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|34.8165|137.248056}}|caption=Yatsurugi Shrine|width=256}} '''Yatsurugi Shrine''' (やつるぎ じんじゃ, Yatsurugi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in Miyacho, [[Gamagori City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]. The shrine is classified as a former [[Gōsha]] and is known for hosting the {{ill|Miya Matsuri|ja|三谷祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == History == According to the [[Kuni no Myojin Meisho Chō]], the shrine is listed as "Gojōi no Kami, Yatsurugi Tenjin, located in Hōge District." The shrine is an ancient shrine that holds a deep history of enshrining a spirit originally residing in [[Atsuta Shrine]] in [[Aichi District]], [[Owari Province]]. Initially, Yatsurugi Shrine was the guardian deity of the Miyacho area, worshipped by the locals and revered as a war deity by successive warriors. The shrine is said to have been established in [[1089]] (Kanji 3). It fell into disrepair at one point, but in [[1190]] (Kenkyū 1), on the orders of [[Minamoto no Yoritomo]], [[Adachi Tōkurō Morinaga]] oversaw the reconstruction of the shrine buildings. [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]] visited the shrine to pray for success in battle during his travels to [[Suruga Province]] in [[1548]] (Tenbun 16) and again in [[1582]] (Tenshō 10) on his return to [[Hamamatsu]]. In [[1600]] (Keichō 5), the local lord donated shrine lands. Subsequent lords continued this practice, which continued until the [[Meiji Restoration]]. The records in "Mikawa Futamatsukigami" state that Yatsurugi Daimyōjin Shrine in Miyacho held six koku, six to, four shō, and six gō of land. In [[1885]] (Meiji 17), the shrine was officially designated as a [[Gōsha]]. The shrine grounds once covered an area of four chō and four faces, but were reduced during the [[Sengoku period]]. However, the current precincts still cover over 1800 tsubo, with old pine trees surrounding the shrine buildings, evoking a sense of ancient times. The shrine grounds include several subsidiary shrines: Genta Shrine, Tenman Shrine, Suwa Shrine, Inari Shrine, Mikwa Shrine, Akiba Shrine, Shakuchi Shrine, Suijin Shrine, and Konpira Shrine. == Miya festival == {{See also|Miya festival}} [[File:Miyamatsuri.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Sea procession of the {{ill|Miya Matsuri|ja|三谷祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}]] The {{ill|Miya Matsuri|ja|三谷祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is a famous local event known for its grand procession of splendid floats and traditional performances. The festival, celebrated at both Yatsurugi Shrine and Wakamiya Shrine in Miyacho, is said to have started in [[1696]] (Genroku 9) when the village headman Sazaemon received a divine revelation in a dream to conduct a mikoshi procession. The festival, which involves the entire population of Miyacho, has preserved its ancient rituals to the present day. Highlights include the raising of flags, a grand float parade modeled after the [[Gion festival]] in Kyoto, and various dedication dances from each district. The scale and disciplined organization of the festival make it a unique event. The traditional sea procession of the floats was discontinued in [[1961]] (Shōwa 36) due to land reclamation but was revived in [[1995]] (Heisei 8) to commemorate the 300th anniversary of the festival's inception. == annual Events == * New Year's festival - [[January 1]] * National Foundation festival - [[February 11]] * Prayer festival - 1st [[Sunday]] of [[February]] * festival - 3rd and 4th [[Saturday]] and [[Sunday]] of [[October]] * Shichi-Go-San (Children's festival) - Mid-October to [[November 30]] * Great Purification - [[December 31]] * New Year's Eve festival - December 31 == Subordinate Shrines == === Subsidiary Shrine === * Genta Shrine - deity: [[Otoyo no Mikoto]] === Subordinate Shrines === * Tenman Shrine - deity: [[Sugawara no Michizane]] * Suwa Shrine - deity: [[Takeminakata no Ōkami]] * Inari Shrine - deity: [[Ukanomitama no Ōkami]] * Akiba Shrine - deity: [[Kagutsuchi|Hi no Kagutsuchi no Ōkami]] * Mikwa Shrine - deity: [[Toyouke Ōmikami]] * Shakuchi Shrine - deity: [[Saruhiko Ōkami]] * Konpira Shrine - deity: [[Ōmononushi no Ōkami]] * Suijin Shrine - deity: [[Mizuhanome no Ōkami]] == Precincts == File:Le Temple Shintô Yatsurugi-jinja - Le torii d'entrée.jpg|Stone torii gate at the entrance File:Le Temple Shintô Yatsurugi-jinja - Le temizuya.jpg|Chōzuya (purification fountain) File:Le Temple Shintô Yatsurugi-jinja - Le Temple Shintô Ten'man-sha.jpg|Tenman Shrine (subordinate shrine) File:Le Temple Shintô Yatsurugi-jinja - Le Temple Shintô Suijin-gû.jpg|Suijin Shrine (subordinate shrine) == Access == * About a 10-minute walk from [[Mikawa Mitani Station]] on the [[Tōkaidō Main Line]] ([[Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]]). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Yatsurugi-jinja (Gamagori, Aichi)}} * [http://yaturugijinjya.com/ Yatsurugi Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.38fes.jp/ Miya festival Official Website] * [https://www.city.gamagori.lg.jp/unit/kankoshoko/miyamaturi.html Miya festival] - Official website of Gamagori City * [http://www.gamagori.jp/look/detail_56_1_10_0_0.html Miya festival] - Gamagori Tourism Association * [http://www.miyaspa.com/miyamatsuri/ Miya festival - The Extraordinary festival] - Miya Onsen * [http://www.aichi-kanko.jp/search/detail.asp?id=1543 Miya festival] - Aichi Prefecture Tourism Guide * {{Youtube|kO9SSWMHxTo|The Extraordinary Miya festival - Gamagori City}} - [[This is Mikawa Broadcasting Station]] * {{Facebook|38fes|Miya festival}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} ]]]] {{translated page|ja|八劔神社 (蒲郡市)|version=99684594|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Gamagori City]] [[Category:Buildings in Gamagori City]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Yatsurugi Shrine]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[sn:Yatsurugi Shrine]][[sn:Yatsurugi Shrine]]}} [[simple:Yatsurugi Shrine]] [[sn:Yatsurugi Shrine]] [[en:Yatsurugi Shrine]] [[ja:八劔神社 (蒲郡市)]]

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image=Gamagori city Yatsurugi-jinja Shaden.jpg|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Yatsurugi Shrine | image=[[File:Gamagori city Yatsurugi-jinja Shaden.jpg|250px]]
Worship Hall | location=[[Aichi Prefecture]], [[Gamagori City]], [[Miyacho]] Nanaho 139 | coordinates={{Coord|34|48|59.4|N|137|14|53.0|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Yatsurugi Shrine (Gamagori City)}} | deity=[[Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru no Mikoto]] | type=Former [[Gōsha]] | established=[[Kanji (era)|Kanji]] 3 (1089) | 札所等= | festival=3rd and 4th [[Saturday]] and [[Sunday]] of [[October]] }} [[File:Miyamatsuri.JPG|link=|thumb|220x220px|Underwater [[shinko-shiki]]]]Yatsurugi-jinja (八劔神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] famous for its underwater [[Shinko-shiki]]. It has floating {{ill|mikoshi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mikoshi|qq=}} that are carried by people across the water in a parade.{{Cite web|url=https://morethanrelo.com/en/floating-mikoshi-miya-festival/|access-date=2023-04-12|website=morethanrelo.com}} {{出典の明記|date=2020th year 4月}} {{統合文字|劔}} {{location map|Japan mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|34.8165|137.248056}}|caption=Yatsurugi Shrine|width=256}} '''Yatsurugi Shrine''' (やつるぎ じんじゃ, Yatsurugi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in Miyacho, [[Gamagori City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]. The shrine is classified as a former [[Gōsha]] and is known for hosting the {{ill|Miya Matsuri|ja|三谷祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miya Matsuri|qq=}}. == History == According to the [[Kuni no Myojin Meisho Chō]], the shrine is listed as "Gojōi no Kami, Yatsurugi Tenjin, located in Hōge District." The shrine is an ancient shrine that holds a deep history of enshrining a spirit originally residing in [[Atsuta Shrine]] in [[Aichi District]], [[Owari Province]]. Initially, Yatsurugi Shrine was the guardian deity of the Miyacho area, worshipped by the locals and revered as a war deity by successive warriors. The shrine is said to have been established in [[1089]] (Kanji 3). It fell into disrepair at one point, but in [[1190]] (Kenkyū 1), on the orders of [[Minamoto no Yoritomo]], [[Adachi Tōkurō Morinaga]] oversaw the reconstruction of the shrine buildings. [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]] visited the shrine to pray for success in battle during his travels to [[Suruga Province]] in [[1548]] (Tenbun 16) and again in [[1582]] (Tenshō 10) on his return to [[Hamamatsu]]. In [[1600]] (Keichō 5), the local lord donated shrine lands. Subsequent lords continued this practice, which continued until the [[Meiji Restoration]]. The records in "Mikawa Futamatsukigami" state that Yatsurugi Daimyōjin Shrine in Miyacho held six koku, six to, four shō, and six gō of land. In [[1885]] (Meiji 17), the shrine was officially designated as a [[Gōsha]]. The shrine grounds once covered an area of four chō and four faces, but were reduced during the [[Sengoku period]]. However, the current precincts still cover over 1800 tsubo, with old pine trees surrounding the shrine buildings, evoking a sense of ancient times. The shrine grounds include several subsidiary shrines: Genta Shrine, Tenman Shrine, Suwa Shrine, Inari Shrine, Mikwa Shrine, Akiba Shrine, Shakuchi Shrine, Suijin Shrine, and Konpira Shrine. == Miya festival == {{See also|Miya festival}} [[File:Miyamatsuri.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Sea procession of the {{ill|Miya Matsuri|ja|三谷祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miya Matsuri|qq=}}]] The {{ill|Miya Matsuri|ja|三谷祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miya Matsuri|qq=}} is a famous local event known for its grand procession of splendid floats and traditional performances. The festival, celebrated at both Yatsurugi Shrine and Wakamiya Shrine in Miyacho, is said to have started in [[1696]] (Genroku 9) when the village headman Sazaemon received a divine revelation in a dream to conduct a mikoshi procession. The festival, which involves the entire population of Miyacho, has preserved its ancient rituals to the present day. Highlights include the raising of flags, a grand float parade modeled after the [[Gion festival]] in Kyoto, and various dedication dances from each district. The scale and disciplined organization of the festival make it a unique event. The traditional sea procession of the floats was discontinued in [[1961]] (Shōwa 36) due to land reclamation but was revived in [[1995]] (Heisei 8) to commemorate the 300th anniversary of the festival's inception. == annual Events == * New Year's festival - [[January 1]] * National Foundation festival - [[February 11]] * Prayer festival - 1st [[Sunday]] of [[February]] * festival - 3rd and 4th [[Saturday]] and [[Sunday]] of [[October]] * Shichi-Go-San (Children's festival) - Mid-October to [[November 30]] * Great Purification - [[December 31]] * New Year's Eve festival - December 31 == Subordinate Shrines == === Subsidiary Shrine === * Genta Shrine - deity: [[Otoyo no Mikoto]] === Subordinate Shrines === * Tenman Shrine - deity: [[Sugawara no Michizane]] * Suwa Shrine - deity: [[Takeminakata no Ōkami]] * Inari Shrine - deity: [[Ukanomitama no Ōkami]] * Akiba Shrine - deity: [[Kagu-tsuchi|Hi no Kagutsuchi no Ōkami]] * Mikwa Shrine - deity: [[Toyouke Ōmikami]] * Shakuchi Shrine - deity: [[Saruhiko Ōkami]] * Konpira Shrine - deity: [[Ōmononushi no Ōkami]] * Suijin Shrine - deity: [[Mizuhanome no Ōkami]] == Precincts == File:Le Temple Shintô Yatsurugi-jinja - Le torii d'entrée.jpg|Stone torii gate at the entrance File:Le Temple Shintô Yatsurugi-jinja - Le temizuya.jpg|Chōzuya (purification fountain) File:Le Temple Shintô Yatsurugi-jinja - Le Temple Shintô Ten'man-sha.jpg|Tenman Shrine (subordinate shrine) File:Le Temple Shintô Yatsurugi-jinja - Le Temple Shintô Suijin-gû.jpg|Suijin Shrine (subordinate shrine) == Access == * About a 10-minute walk from [[Mikawa Mitani Station]] on the [[Tōkaidō Main Line]] ([[Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]]). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Yatsurugi-jinja (Gamagori, Aichi)}} * [http://yaturugijinjya.com/ Yatsurugi Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.38fes.jp/ Miya festival Official Website] * [https://www.city.gamagori.lg.jp/unit/kankoshoko/miyamaturi.html Miya festival] - Official website of Gamagori City * [http://www.gamagori.jp/look/detail_56_1_10_0_0.html Miya festival] - Gamagori Tourism Association * [http://www.miyaspa.com/miyamatsuri/ Miya festival - The Extraordinary festival] - Miya Onsen * [http://www.aichi-kanko.jp/search/detail.asp?id=1543 Miya festival] - Aichi Prefecture Tourism Guide * {{Youtube|kO9SSWMHxTo|The Extraordinary Miya festival - Gamagori City}} - [[This is Mikawa Broadcasting Station]] * {{Facebook|38fes|Miya festival}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} ]]]] {{translated page|ja|八劔神社 (蒲郡市)|version=99684594|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Gamagori City]] [[Category:Buildings in Gamagori City]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1]] [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Yatsurugi Shrine]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[sn:Yatsurugi Shrine]][[sn:Yatsurugi Shrine]]}} [[simple:Yatsurugi Shrine]] [[sn:Yatsurugi Shrine]] [[en:Yatsurugi Shrine]] [[ja:八劔神社 (蒲郡市)]]

The shrine is an ancient shrine that holds a deep history of enshrining a spirit originally residing in [[Atsuta Shrine]] in [[Aichi District]], [[Owari Province]]. Initially, Yatsurugi Shrine was the guardian deity of the Miyacho area, worshipped by the locals and revered as a war deity by successive warriors. The shrine is said to have been established in [[1089]] (Kanji 3). It fell into disrepair at one point, but in [[1190]] (Kenkyū 1), on the orders of [[Minamoto no Yoritomo]], [[Adachi Tōkurō Morinaga]] oversaw the reconstruction of the shrine buildings. [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]] visited the shrine to pray for success in battle during his travels to [[Suruga Province]] in [[1548]] (Tenbun 16) and again in [[1582]] (Tenshō 10) on his return to [[Hamamatsu]]. In [[1600]] (Keichō 5), the local lord donated shrine lands. Subsequent lords continued this practice, which continued until the [[Meiji Restoration]]. The records in "Mikawa Futamatsukigami" state that Yatsurugi Daimyōjin Shrine in Miyacho held six koku, six to, four shō, and six gō of land. In [[1885]] (Meiji 17), the shrine was officially designated as a [[Gōsha]]. The shrine grounds once covered an area of four chō and four faces, but were reduced during the [[Sengoku period]]. However, the current precincts still cover over 1800 tsubo, with old pine trees surrounding the shrine buildings, evoking a sense of ancient times. The shrine grounds include several subsidiary shrines: Genta Shrine, Tenman Shrine, Suwa Shrine, Inari Shrine, Mikwa Shrine, Akiba Shrine, Shakuchi Shrine, Suijin Shrine, and Konpira Shrine. == Miya festival == {{See also|Miya festival}} [[File:Miyamatsuri.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Sea procession of the {{ill|Miya Matsuri|ja|三谷祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miya Matsuri|qq=}}]] The {{ill|Miya Matsuri|ja|三谷祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miya Matsuri|qq=}} is a famous local event known for its grand procession of splendid floats and traditional performances. The festival, celebrated at both Yatsurugi Shrine and Wakamiya Shrine in Miyacho, is said to have started in [[1696]] (Genroku 9) when the village headman Sazaemon received a divine revelation in a dream to conduct a mikoshi procession. The festival, which involves the entire population of Miyacho, has preserved its ancient rituals to the present day. Highlights include the raising of flags, a grand float parade modeled after the [[Gion festival]] in Kyoto, and various dedication dances from each district. The scale and disciplined organization of the festival make it a unique event. The traditional sea procession of the floats was discontinued in [[1961]] (Shōwa 36) due to land reclamation but was revived in [[1995]] (Heisei 8) to commemorate the 300th anniversary of the festival's inception. == annual Events == * New Year's festival - [[January 1]] * National Foundation festival - [[February 11]] * Prayer festival - 1st [[Sunday]] of [[February]] * festival - 3rd and 4th [[Saturday]] and [[Sunday]] of [[October]] * Shichi-Go-San (Children's festival) - Mid-October to [[November 30]] * Great Purification - [[December 31]] * New Year's Eve festival - December 31 == Subordinate Shrines == === Subsidiary Shrine === * Genta Shrine - deity: [[Otoyo no Mikoto]] === Subordinate Shrines === * Tenman Shrine - deity: [[Sugawara no Michizane]] * Suwa Shrine - deity: [[Takeminakata no Ōkami]] * Inari Shrine - deity: [[Ukanomitama no Ōkami]] * Akiba Shrine - deity: [[Kagu-tsuchi|Hi no Kagutsuchi no Ōkami]] * Mikwa Shrine - deity: [[Toyouke Ōmikami]] * Shakuchi Shrine - deity: [[Saruhiko Ōkami]] * Konpira Shrine - deity: [[Ōmononushi no Ōkami]] * Suijin Shrine - deity: [[Mizuhanome no Ōkami]] == Precincts == File:Le Temple Shintô Yatsurugi-jinja - Le torii d'entrée.jpg|Stone torii gate at the entrance File:Le Temple Shintô Yatsurugi-jinja - Le temizuya.jpg|Chōzuya (purification fountain) File:Le Temple Shintô Yatsurugi-jinja - Le Temple Shintô Ten'man-sha.jpg|Tenman Shrine (subordinate shrine) File:Le Temple Shintô Yatsurugi-jinja - Le Temple Shintô Suijin-gû.jpg|Suijin Shrine (subordinate shrine) == Access == * About a 10-minute walk from [[Mikawa Mitani Station]] on the [[Tōkaidō Main Line]] ([[Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]]). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Yatsurugi-jinja (Gamagori, Aichi)}} * [http://yaturugijinjya.com/ Yatsurugi Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.38fes.jp/ Miya festival Official Website] * [https://www.city.gamagori.lg.jp/unit/kankoshoko/miyamaturi.html Miya festival] - Official website of Gamagori City * [http://www.gamagori.jp/look/detail_56_1_10_0_0.html Miya festival] - Gamagori Tourism Association * [http://www.miyaspa.com/miyamatsuri/ Miya festival - The Extraordinary festival] - Miya Onsen * [http://www.aichi-kanko.jp/search/detail.asp?id=1543 Miya festival] - Aichi Prefecture Tourism Guide * {{Youtube|kO9SSWMHxTo|The Extraordinary Miya festival - Gamagori City}} - [[This is Mikawa Broadcasting Station]] * {{Facebook|38fes|Miya festival}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} ]]]] {{translated page|ja|八劔神社 (蒲郡市)|version=99684594|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Gamagori City]] [[Category:Buildings in Gamagori City]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1]] [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[sn:Yatsurugi Shrine]][[sn:Yatsurugi Shrine]]}} [[simple:Yatsurugi Shrine]] [[sn:Yatsurugi Shrine]] [[en:Yatsurugi Shrine]] [[ja:八劔神社 (蒲郡市)]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Gamagōri]]

In [[1885]] (Meiji 17), the shrine was officially designated as a [[Gōsha]]. The shrine grounds once covered an area of four chō and four faces, but were reduced during the [[Sengoku period]]. However, the current precincts still cover over 1800 tsubo, with old pine trees surrounding the shrine buildings, evoking a sense of ancient times. The shrine grounds include several subsidiary shrines: Genta Shrine, Tenman Shrine, Suwa Shrine, Inari Shrine, Mikwa Shrine, Akiba Shrine, Shakuchi Shrine, Suijin Shrine, and Konpira Shrine. == Miya festival == {{See also|Miya festival}} [[File:Miyamatsuri.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Sea procession of the {{ill|Miya Matsuri|ja|三谷祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miya Matsuri|qq=}}]] The {{ill|Miya Matsuri|ja|三谷祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miya Matsuri|qq=}} is a famous local event known for its grand procession of splendid floats and traditional performances. The festival, celebrated at both Yatsurugi Shrine and Wakamiya Shrine in Miyacho, is said to have started in [[1696]] (Genroku 9) when the village headman Sazaemon received a divine revelation in a dream to conduct a mikoshi procession. Worship Hall | location=[[Aichi Prefecture]], [[Gamagori City]], [[Miyacho]] Nanaho 139 | coordinates={{Coord|34|48|59.4|N|137|14|53.0|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Yatsurugi Shrine (Gamagori City)}} | deity=[[Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru no Mikoto]] | type=Former [[Gōsha]] | established=[[Kanji (era)|Kanji]] 3 (1089) | 札所等= | festival=3rd and 4th [[Saturday]] and [[Sunday]] of [[October]] }} [[File:Miyamatsuri.JPG|link=|thumb|220x220px|Underwater [[shinko-shiki]]]]Yatsurugi-jinja (八劔神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] famous for its underwater [[Shinko-shiki]]. It has floating {{ill|mikoshi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mikoshi|qq=}} that are carried by people across the water in a parade.{{Cite web|url=https://morethanrelo.com/en/floating-mikoshi-miya-festival/|access-date=2023-04-12|website=morethanrelo.com}} {{出典の明記|date=2020th year 4月}} {{統合文字|劔}} {{location map|Japan mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|34.8165|137.248056}}|caption=Yatsurugi Shrine|width=256}} '''Yatsurugi Shrine''' (やつるぎ じんじゃ, Yatsurugi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in Miyacho, [[Gamagori City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]. The shrine is classified as a former [[Gōsha]] and is known for hosting the {{ill|Miya Matsuri|ja|三谷祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miya Matsuri|qq=}}. == History == According to the [[Kuni no Myojin Meisho Chō]], the shrine is listed as "Gojōi no Kami, Yatsurugi Tenjin, located in Hōge District." The shrine is an ancient shrine that holds a deep history of enshrining a spirit originally residing in [[Atsuta Shrine]] in [[Aichi District]], [[Owari Province]]. Initially, Yatsurugi Shrine was the guardian deity of the Miyacho area, worshipped by the locals and revered as a war deity by successive warriors. The shrine is said to have been established in [[1089]] (Kanji 3). It fell into disrepair at one point, but in [[1190]] (Kenkyū 1), on the orders of [[Minamoto no Yoritomo]], [[Adachi Tōkurō Morinaga]] oversaw the reconstruction of the shrine buildings. [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]] visited the shrine to pray for success in battle during his travels to [[Suruga Province]] in [[1548]] (Tenbun 16) and again in [[1582]] (Tenshō 10) on his return to [[Hamamatsu]]. In [[1600]] (Keichō 5), the local lord donated shrine lands. Subsequent lords continued this practice, which continued until the [[Meiji Restoration]]. The records in "Mikawa Futamatsukigami" state that Yatsurugi Daimyōjin Shrine in Miyacho held six koku, six to, four shō, and six gō of land. In [[1885]] (Meiji 17), the shrine was officially designated as a [[Gōsha]]. The shrine grounds once covered an area of four chō and four faces, but were reduced during the [[Sengoku period]]. However, the current precincts still cover over 1800 tsubo, with old pine trees surrounding the shrine buildings, evoking a sense of ancient times. The shrine grounds include several subsidiary shrines: Genta Shrine, Tenman Shrine, Suwa Shrine, Inari Shrine, Mikwa Shrine, Akiba Shrine, Shakuchi Shrine, Suijin Shrine, and Konpira Shrine. == Miya festival == {{See also|Miya festival}} [[File:Miyamatsuri.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Sea procession of the {{ill|Miya Matsuri|ja|三谷祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miya Matsuri|qq=}}]] The {{ill|Miya Matsuri|ja|三谷祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miya Matsuri|qq=}} is a famous local event known for its grand procession of splendid floats and traditional performances. The festival, celebrated at both Yatsurugi Shrine and Wakamiya Shrine in Miyacho, is said to have started in [[1696]] (Genroku 9) when the village headman Sazaemon received a divine revelation in a dream to conduct a mikoshi procession. The festival, which involves the entire population of Miyacho, has preserved its ancient rituals to the present day. Highlights include the raising of flags, a grand float parade modeled after the [[Gion festival]] in Kyoto, and various dedication dances from each district. The scale and disciplined organization of the festival make it a unique event. The traditional sea procession of the floats was discontinued in [[1961]] (Shōwa 36) due to land reclamation but was revived in [[1995]] (Heisei 8) to commemorate the 300th anniversary of the festival's inception. == annual Events == * New Year's festival - [[January 1]] * National Foundation festival - [[February 11]] * Prayer festival - 1st [[Sunday]] of [[February]] * festival - 3rd and 4th [[Saturday]] and [[Sunday]] of [[October]] * Shichi-Go-San (Children's festival) - Mid-October to [[November 30]] * Great Purification - [[December 31]] * New Year's Eve festival - December 31 == Subordinate Shrines == === Subsidiary Shrine === * Genta Shrine - deity: [[Otoyo no Mikoto]] === Subordinate Shrines === * Tenman Shrine - deity: [[Sugawara no Michizane]] * Suwa Shrine - deity: [[Takeminakata no Ōkami]] * Inari Shrine - deity: [[Ukanomitama no Ōkami]] * Akiba Shrine - deity: [[Kagu-tsuchi|Hi no Kagutsuchi no Ōkami]] * Mikwa Shrine - deity: [[Toyouke Ōmikami]] * Shakuchi Shrine - deity: [[Saruhiko Ōkami]] * Konpira Shrine - deity: [[Ōmononushi no Ōkami]] * Suijin Shrine - deity: [[Mizuhanome no Ōkami]] == Precincts == File:Le Temple Shintô Yatsurugi-jinja - Le torii d'entrée.jpg|Stone torii gate at the entrance File:Le Temple Shintô Yatsurugi-jinja - Le temizuya.jpg|Chōzuya (purification fountain) File:Le Temple Shintô Yatsurugi-jinja - Le Temple Shintô Ten'man-sha.jpg|Tenman Shrine (subordinate shrine) File:Le Temple Shintô Yatsurugi-jinja - Le Temple Shintô Suijin-gû.jpg|Suijin Shrine (subordinate shrine) == Access == * About a 10-minute walk from [[Mikawa Mitani Station]] on the [[Tōkaidō Main Line]] ([[Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]]). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Yatsurugi-jinja (Gamagori, Aichi)}} * [http://yaturugijinjya.com/ Yatsurugi Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.38fes.jp/ Miya festival Official Website] * [https://www.city.gamagori.lg.jp/unit/kankoshoko/miyamaturi.html Miya festival] - Official website of Gamagori City * [http://www.gamagori.jp/look/detail_56_1_10_0_0.html Miya festival] - Gamagori Tourism Association * [http://www.miyaspa.com/miyamatsuri/ Miya festival - The Extraordinary festival] - Miya Onsen * [http://www.aichi-kanko.jp/search/detail.asp?id=1543 Miya festival] - Aichi Prefecture Tourism Guide * {{Youtube|kO9SSWMHxTo|The Extraordinary Miya festival - Gamagori City}} - [[This is Mikawa Broadcasting Station]] * {{Facebook|38fes|Miya festival}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} ]]]] {{translated page|ja|八劔神社 (蒲郡市)|version=99684594|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Gamagori City]] [[Category:Buildings in Gamagori City]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1]] [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[sn:Yatsurugi Shrine]][[sn:Yatsurugi Shrine]]}} [[simple:Yatsurugi Shrine]] [[sn:Yatsurugi Shrine]] [[ja:八劔神社 (蒲郡市)]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Gamagōri]] [[Category:History of Gamagori]] {{Commons category|Yatsurugi-jinja (Gamagori, Aichi)}}

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image=Gamagori city Yatsurugi-jinja Shaden.jpg|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Yatsurugi Shrine | image=[[File:Gamagori city Yatsurugi-jinja Shaden.jpg|250px]]
Worship Hall | location=[[Aichi Prefecture]], [[Gamagori City]], [[Miyacho]] Nanaho 139 | coordinates={{Coord|34|48|59.4|N|137|14|53.0|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Yatsurugi Shrine (Gamagori City)}} | deity=[[Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru no Mikoto]] | type=Former [[Gōsha]] | established=[[Kanji (era)|Kanji]] 3 (1089) | 札所等= | festival=3rd and 4th [[Saturday]] and [[Sunday]] of [[October]] }} [[File:Miyamatsuri.JPG|link=|thumb|220x220px|Underwater [[shinko-shiki]]]]Yatsurugi-jinja (八劔神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] famous for its underwater [[Shinko-shiki]]. It has floating {{ill|mikoshi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mikoshi|qq=}} that are carried by people across the water in a parade.{{Cite web|url=https://morethanrelo.com/en/floating-mikoshi-miya-festival/|access-date=2023-04-12|website=morethanrelo.com}} {{出典の明記|date=2020th year 4月}} {{統合文字|劔}} {{location map|Japan mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|34.8165|137.248056}}|caption=Yatsurugi Shrine|width=256}} '''Yatsurugi Shrine''' (やつるぎ じんじゃ, Yatsurugi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in Miyacho, [[Gamagori City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]. The shrine is classified as a former [[Gōsha]] and is known for hosting the {{ill|Miya Matsuri|ja|三谷祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miya Matsuri|qq=}}. == History == According to the [[Kuni no Myojin Meisho Chō]], the shrine is listed as "Gojōi no Kami, Yatsurugi Tenjin, located in Hōge District." The shrine is an ancient shrine that holds a deep history of enshrining a spirit originally residing in [[Atsuta Shrine]] in [[Aichi District]], [[Owari Province]]. Initially, Yatsurugi Shrine was the guardian deity of the Miyacho area, worshipped by the locals and revered as a war deity by successive warriors. The shrine is said to have been established in [[:en:1089|1089]] (Kanji 3). It fell into disrepair at one point, but in [[:en:1190|1190]] (Kenkyū 1), on the orders of [[Minamoto no Yoritomo]], [[Adachi Tōkurō Morinaga]] oversaw the reconstruction of the shrine buildings. [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]] visited the shrine to pray for success in battle during his travels to [[Suruga Province]] in [[:en:1548|1548]] (Tenbun 16) and again in [[1582]] (Tenshō 10) on his return to [[Hamamatsu]]. In [[1600]] (Keichō 5), the local lord donated shrine lands. Subsequent lords continued this practice, which continued until the [[Meiji Restoration]]. The records in "Mikawa Futamatsukigami" state that Yatsurugi Daimyōjin Shrine in Miyacho held six koku, six to, four shō, and six gō of land. In [[1885]] (Meiji 17), the shrine was officially designated as a [[Gōsha]]. The shrine grounds once covered an area of four chō and four faces, but were reduced during the [[Sengoku period]]. However, the current precincts still cover over 1800 tsubo, with old pine trees surrounding the shrine buildings, evoking a sense of ancient times. The shrine grounds include several subsidiary shrines: Genta Shrine, Tenman Shrine, Suwa Shrine, Inari Shrine, Mikwa Shrine, Akiba Shrine, Shakuchi Shrine, Suijin Shrine, and Konpira Shrine. == Miya festival == {{See also|Miya festival}} [[File:Miyamatsuri.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Sea procession of the {{ill|Miya Matsuri|ja|三谷祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miya Matsuri|qq=}}]] The {{ill|Miya Matsuri|ja|三谷祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miya Matsuri|qq=}} is a famous local event known for its grand procession of splendid floats and traditional performances. The festival, celebrated at both Yatsurugi Shrine and Wakamiya Shrine in Miyacho, is said to have started in [[1696]] (Genroku 9) when the village headman Sazaemon received a divine revelation in a dream to conduct a mikoshi procession. The festival, which involves the entire population of Miyacho, has preserved its ancient rituals to the present day. Highlights include the raising of flags, a grand float parade modeled after the [[Gion festival]] in Kyoto, and various dedication dances from each district. The scale and disciplined organization of the festival make it a unique event. The traditional sea procession of the floats was discontinued in [[1961]] (Shōwa 36) due to land reclamation but was revived in [[1995]] (Heisei 8) to commemorate the 300th anniversary of the festival's inception. == annual Events == * New Year's festival - [[January 1]] * National Foundation festival - [[February 11]] * Prayer festival - 1st [[Sunday]] of [[February]] * festival - 3rd and 4th [[Saturday]] and [[Sunday]] of [[October]] * Shichi-Go-San (Children's festival) - Mid-October to [[November 30]] * Great Purification - [[December 31]] * New Year's Eve festival - December 31 == Subordinate Shrines == === Subsidiary Shrine === * Genta Shrine - deity: [[Otoyo no Mikoto]] === Subordinate Shrines === * Tenman Shrine - deity: [[Sugawara no Michizane]] * Suwa Shrine - deity: [[Takeminakata no Ōkami]] * Inari Shrine - deity: [[Ukanomitama no Ōkami]] * Akiba Shrine - deity: [[Kagu-tsuchi|Hi no Kagutsuchi no Ōkami]] * Mikwa Shrine - deity: [[Toyouke Ōmikami]] * Shakuchi Shrine - deity: [[Saruhiko Ōkami]] * Konpira Shrine - deity: [[Ōmononushi no Ōkami]] * Suijin Shrine - deity: [[Mizuhanome no Ōkami]] == Precincts == File:Le Temple Shintô Yatsurugi-jinja - Le torii d'entrée.jpg|Stone torii gate at the entrance File:Le Temple Shintô Yatsurugi-jinja - Le temizuya.jpg|Chōzuya (purification fountain) File:Le Temple Shintô Yatsurugi-jinja - Le Temple Shintô Ten'man-sha.jpg|Tenman Shrine (subordinate shrine) File:Le Temple Shintô Yatsurugi-jinja - Le Temple Shintô Suijin-gû.jpg|Suijin Shrine (subordinate shrine) == Access == * About a 10-minute walk from [[Mikawa Mitani Station]] on the [[Tōkaidō Main Line]] ([[Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]]). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Yatsurugi-jinja (Gamagori, Aichi)}} * [http://yaturugijinjya.com/ Yatsurugi Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.38fes.jp/ Miya festival Official Website] * [https://www.city.gamagori.lg.jp/unit/kankoshoko/miyamaturi.html Miya festival] - Official website of Gamagori City * [http://www.gamagori.jp/look/detail_56_1_10_0_0.html Miya festival] - Gamagori Tourism Association * [http://www.miyaspa.com/miyamatsuri/ Miya festival - The Extraordinary festival] - Miya Onsen * [http://www.aichi-kanko.jp/search/detail.asp?id=1543 Miya festival] - Aichi Prefecture Tourism Guide * {{Youtube|kO9SSWMHxTo|The Extraordinary Miya festival - Gamagori City}} - [[This is Mikawa Broadcasting Station]] * {{Facebook|38fes|Miya festival}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} ]]]] {{translated page|ja|八劔神社 (蒲郡市)|version=99684594|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Gamagori City]] [[Category:Buildings in Gamagori City]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1]] [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[sn:Yatsurugi Shrine]][[sn:Yatsurugi Shrine]]}} [[simple:Yatsurugi Shrine]] [[sn:Yatsurugi Shrine]] [[ja:八劔神社 (蒲郡市)]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Gamagōri]] [[Category:History of Gamagori]] {{Commons category|Yatsurugi-jinja (Gamagori, Aichi)}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image=Gamagori city Yatsurugi-jinja Shaden.jpg|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Yatsurugi Shrine | image=[[File:Gamagori city Yatsurugi-jinja Shaden.jpg|250px]]
Worship Hall | location=[[Aichi Prefecture]], [[Gamagori City]], [[Miyacho]] Nanaho 139 | coordinates={{Coord|34|48|59.4|N|137|14|53.0|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Yatsurugi Shrine (Gamagori City)}} | deity=[[Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru no Mikoto]] | type=Former [[Gōsha]] | established=[[Kanji (era)|Kanji]] 3 (1089) | 札所等= | festival=3rd and 4th [[Saturday]] and [[Sunday]] of [[October]] }} [[File:Miyamatsuri.JPG|link=|thumb|220x220px|Underwater [[shinko-shiki]]]]Yatsurugi-jinja (八劔神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] famous for its underwater [[Shinko-shiki]]. It has floating {{ill|mikoshi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mikoshi|qq=}} that are carried by people across the water in a parade.{{Cite web|url=https://morethanrelo.com/en/floating-mikoshi-miya-festival/|access-date=2023-04-12|website=morethanrelo.com}} {{出典の明記|date=2020th year 4月}} {{統合文字|劔}} {{location map|Japan mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|34.8165|137.248056}}|caption=Yatsurugi Shrine|width=256}} '''Yatsurugi Shrine''' (やつるぎ じんじゃ, Yatsurugi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in Miyacho, [[Gamagori City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]. The shrine is classified as a former [[Gōsha]] and is known for hosting the {{ill|Miya Matsuri|ja|三谷祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miya Matsuri|qq=}}. == History == According to the [[Kuni no Myojin Meisho Chō]], the shrine is listed as "Gojōi no Kami, Yatsurugi Tenjin, located in Hōge District." The shrine is an ancient shrine that holds a deep history of enshrining a spirit originally residing in [[Atsuta Shrine]] in [[Aichi District]], [[Owari Province]]. Initially, Yatsurugi Shrine was the guardian deity of the Miyacho area, worshipped by the locals and revered as a war deity by successive warriors. The shrine is said to have been established in [[:en:1089|1089]] (Kanji 3). It fell into disrepair at one point, but in [[:en:1190|1190]] (Kenkyū 1), on the orders of [[Minamoto no Yoritomo]], [[Adachi Tōkurō Morinaga]] oversaw the reconstruction of the shrine buildings. [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]] visited the shrine to pray for success in battle during his travels to [[Suruga Province]] in [[:en:1548|1548]] (Tenbun 16) and again in [[:en:1582|1582]] (Tenshō 10) on his return to [[Hamamatsu]]. In [[:en:1600|1600]] (Keichō 5), the local lord donated shrine lands. Subsequent lords continued this practice, which continued until the [[Meiji Restoration]]. The records in "Mikawa Futamatsukigami" state that Yatsurugi Daimyōjin Shrine in Miyacho held six koku, six to, four shō, and six gō of land. In [[1885]] (Meiji 17), the shrine was officially designated as a [[Gōsha]]. The shrine grounds once covered an area of four chō and four faces, but were reduced during the [[Sengoku period]]. However, the current precincts still cover over 1800 tsubo, with old pine trees surrounding the shrine buildings, evoking a sense of ancient times. The shrine grounds include several subsidiary shrines: Genta Shrine, Tenman Shrine, Suwa Shrine, Inari Shrine, Mikwa Shrine, Akiba Shrine, Shakuchi Shrine, Suijin Shrine, and Konpira Shrine. == Miya festival == {{See also|Miya festival}} [[File:Miyamatsuri.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Sea procession of the {{ill|Miya Matsuri|ja|三谷祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miya Matsuri|qq=}}]] The {{ill|Miya Matsuri|ja|三谷祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miya Matsuri|qq=}} is a famous local event known for its grand procession of splendid floats and traditional performances. The festival, celebrated at both Yatsurugi Shrine and Wakamiya Shrine in Miyacho, is said to have started in [[:en:1696|1696]] (Genroku 9) when the village headman Sazaemon received a divine revelation in a dream to conduct a mikoshi procession. The festival, which involves the entire population of Miyacho, has preserved its ancient rituals to the present day. Highlights include the raising of flags, a grand float parade modeled after the [[Gion festival]] in Kyoto, and various dedication dances from each district. The scale and disciplined organization of the festival make it a unique event. The traditional sea procession of the floats was discontinued in [[1961]] (Shōwa 36) due to land reclamation but was revived in [[1995]] (Heisei 8) to commemorate the 300th anniversary of the festival's inception. == annual Events == * New Year's festival - [[January 1]] * National Foundation festival - [[February 11]] * Prayer festival - 1st [[Sunday]] of [[February]] * festival - 3rd and 4th [[Saturday]] and [[Sunday]] of [[October]] * Shichi-Go-San (Children's festival) - Mid-October to [[November 30]] * Great Purification - [[December 31]] * New Year's Eve festival - December 31 == Subordinate Shrines == === Subsidiary Shrine === * Genta Shrine - deity: [[Otoyo no Mikoto]] === Subordinate Shrines === * Tenman Shrine - deity: [[Sugawara no Michizane]] * Suwa Shrine - deity: [[Takeminakata no Ōkami]] * Inari Shrine - deity: [[Ukanomitama no Ōkami]] * Akiba Shrine - deity: [[Kagu-tsuchi|Hi no Kagutsuchi no Ōkami]] * Mikwa Shrine - deity: [[Toyouke Ōmikami]] * Shakuchi Shrine - deity: [[Saruhiko Ōkami]] * Konpira Shrine - deity: [[Ōmononushi no Ōkami]] * Suijin Shrine - deity: [[Mizuhanome no Ōkami]] == Precincts == File:Le Temple Shintô Yatsurugi-jinja - Le torii d'entrée.jpg|Stone torii gate at the entrance File:Le Temple Shintô Yatsurugi-jinja - Le temizuya.jpg|Chōzuya (purification fountain) File:Le Temple Shintô Yatsurugi-jinja - Le Temple Shintô Ten'man-sha.jpg|Tenman Shrine (subordinate shrine) File:Le Temple Shintô Yatsurugi-jinja - Le Temple Shintô Suijin-gû.jpg|Suijin Shrine (subordinate shrine) == Access == * About a 10-minute walk from [[Mikawa Mitani Station]] on the [[Tōkaidō Main Line]] ([[Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]]). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Yatsurugi-jinja (Gamagori, Aichi)}} * [http://yaturugijinjya.com/ Yatsurugi Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.38fes.jp/ Miya festival Official Website] * [https://www.city.gamagori.lg.jp/unit/kankoshoko/miyamaturi.html Miya festival] - Official website of Gamagori City * [http://www.gamagori.jp/look/detail_56_1_10_0_0.html Miya festival] - Gamagori Tourism Association * [http://www.miyaspa.com/miyamatsuri/ Miya festival - The Extraordinary festival] - Miya Onsen * [http://www.aichi-kanko.jp/search/detail.asp?id=1543 Miya festival] - Aichi Prefecture Tourism Guide * {{Youtube|kO9SSWMHxTo|The Extraordinary Miya festival - Gamagori City}} - [[This is Mikawa Broadcasting Station]] * {{Facebook|38fes|Miya festival}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} ]]]] {{translated page|ja|八劔神社 (蒲郡市)|version=99684594|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Gamagori City]] [[Category:Buildings in Gamagori City]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1]] [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[sn:Yatsurugi Shrine]][[sn:Yatsurugi Shrine]]}} [[simple:Yatsurugi Shrine]] [[sn:Yatsurugi Shrine]] [[ja:八劔神社 (蒲郡市)]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Gamagōri]] [[Category:History of Gamagori]] {{Commons category|Yatsurugi-jinja (Gamagori, Aichi)}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image=Gamagori city Yatsurugi-jinja Shaden.jpg|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Yatsurugi Shrine | image=[[File:Gamagori city Yatsurugi-jinja Shaden.jpg|250px]]
Worship Hall | location=[[Aichi Prefecture]], [[Gamagori City]], [[Miyacho]] Nanaho 139 | coordinates={{Coord|34|48|59.4|N|137|14|53.0|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Yatsurugi Shrine (Gamagori City)}} | deity=[[Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru no Mikoto]] | type=Former [[Gōsha]] | established=[[Kanji (era)|Kanji]] 3 (1089) | 札所等= | festival=3rd and 4th [[Saturday]] and [[Sunday]] of [[October]] }} [[File:Miyamatsuri.JPG|link=|thumb|220x220px|Underwater [[shinko-shiki]]]]Yatsurugi-jinja (八劔神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] famous for its underwater [[Shinko-shiki]]. It has floating {{ill|mikoshi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mikoshi|qq=}} that are carried by people across the water in a parade.{{Cite web|url=https://morethanrelo.com/en/floating-mikoshi-miya-festival/|access-date=2023-04-12|website=morethanrelo.com}} {{出典の明記|date=2020th year 4月}} {{統合文字|劔}} {{location map|Japan mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|34.8165|137.248056}}|caption=Yatsurugi Shrine|width=256}} '''Yatsurugi Shrine''' (やつるぎ じんじゃ, Yatsurugi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in Miyacho, [[Gamagori City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]. The shrine is classified as a former [[Gōsha]] and is known for hosting the {{ill|Miya Matsuri|ja|三谷祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miya Matsuri|qq=}}. == History == According to the [[Kuni no Myojin Meisho Chō]], the shrine is listed as "Gojōi no Kami, Yatsurugi Tenjin, located in Hōge District." The shrine is an ancient shrine that holds a deep history of enshrining a spirit originally residing in [[Atsuta Shrine]] in [[Aichi District]], [[Owari Province]]. Initially, Yatsurugi Shrine was the guardian deity of the Miyacho area, worshipped by the locals and revered as a war deity by successive warriors. The shrine is said to have been established in [[:en:1089|1089]] (Kanji 3). It fell into disrepair at one point, but in [[:en:1190|1190]] (Kenkyū 1), on the orders of [[Minamoto no Yoritomo]], [[Adachi Tōkurō Morinaga]] oversaw the reconstruction of the shrine buildings. [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]] visited the shrine to pray for success in battle during his travels to [[Suruga Province]] in [[:en:1548|1548]] (Tenbun 16) and again in [[:en:1582|1582]] (Tenshō 10) on his return to [[Hamamatsu]]. In [[:en:1600|1600]] (Keichō 5), the local lord donated shrine lands. Subsequent lords continued this practice, which continued until the [[Meiji Restoration]]. The records in "Mikawa Futamatsukigami" state that Yatsurugi Daimyōjin Shrine in Miyacho held six koku, six to, four shō, and six gō of land. In [[:en:1885|1885]] (Meiji 17), the shrine was officially designated as a [[Gōsha]]. The shrine grounds once covered an area of four chō and four faces, but were reduced during the [[Sengoku period]]. However, the current precincts still cover over 1800 tsubo, with old pine trees surrounding the shrine buildings, evoking a sense of ancient times. The shrine grounds include several subsidiary shrines: Genta Shrine, Tenman Shrine, Suwa Shrine, Inari Shrine, Mikwa Shrine, Akiba Shrine, Shakuchi Shrine, Suijin Shrine, and Konpira Shrine. == Miya festival == {{See also|Miya festival}} [[File:Miyamatsuri.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Sea procession of the {{ill|Miya Matsuri|ja|三谷祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miya Matsuri|qq=}}]] The {{ill|Miya Matsuri|ja|三谷祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miya Matsuri|qq=}} is a famous local event known for its grand procession of splendid floats and traditional performances. The festival, celebrated at both Yatsurugi Shrine and Wakamiya Shrine in Miyacho, is said to have started in [[:en:1696|1696]] (Genroku 9) when the village headman Sazaemon received a divine revelation in a dream to conduct a mikoshi procession. The festival, which involves the entire population of Miyacho, has preserved its ancient rituals to the present day. Highlights include the raising of flags, a grand float parade modeled after the [[Gion festival]] in Kyoto, and various dedication dances from each district. The scale and disciplined organization of the festival make it a unique event. The traditional sea procession of the floats was discontinued in [[:en:1961|1961]] (Shōwa 36) due to land reclamation but was revived in [[1995]] (Heisei 8) to commemorate the 300th anniversary of the festival's inception. == annual Events == * New Year's festival - [[January 1]] * National Foundation festival - [[February 11]] * Prayer festival - 1st [[Sunday]] of [[February]] * festival - 3rd and 4th [[Saturday]] and [[Sunday]] of [[October]] * Shichi-Go-San (Children's festival) - Mid-October to [[November 30]] * Great Purification - [[December 31]] * New Year's Eve festival - December 31 == Subordinate Shrines == === Subsidiary Shrine === * Genta Shrine - deity: [[Otoyo no Mikoto]] === Subordinate Shrines === * Tenman Shrine - deity: [[Sugawara no Michizane]] * Suwa Shrine - deity: [[Takeminakata no Ōkami]] * Inari Shrine - deity: [[Ukanomitama no Ōkami]] * Akiba Shrine - deity: [[Kagu-tsuchi|Hi no Kagutsuchi no Ōkami]] * Mikwa Shrine - deity: [[Toyouke Ōmikami]] * Shakuchi Shrine - deity: [[Saruhiko Ōkami]] * Konpira Shrine - deity: [[Ōmononushi no Ōkami]] * Suijin Shrine - deity: [[Mizuhanome no Ōkami]] == Precincts == File:Le Temple Shintô Yatsurugi-jinja - Le torii d'entrée.jpg|Stone torii gate at the entrance File:Le Temple Shintô Yatsurugi-jinja - Le temizuya.jpg|Chōzuya (purification fountain) File:Le Temple Shintô Yatsurugi-jinja - Le Temple Shintô Ten'man-sha.jpg|Tenman Shrine (subordinate shrine) File:Le Temple Shintô Yatsurugi-jinja - Le Temple Shintô Suijin-gû.jpg|Suijin Shrine (subordinate shrine) == Access == * About a 10-minute walk from [[Mikawa Mitani Station]] on the [[Tōkaidō Main Line]] ([[Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]]). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Yatsurugi-jinja (Gamagori, Aichi)}} * [http://yaturugijinjya.com/ Yatsurugi Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.38fes.jp/ Miya festival Official Website] * [https://www.city.gamagori.lg.jp/unit/kankoshoko/miyamaturi.html Miya festival] - Official website of Gamagori City * [http://www.gamagori.jp/look/detail_56_1_10_0_0.html Miya festival] - Gamagori Tourism Association * [http://www.miyaspa.com/miyamatsuri/ Miya festival - The Extraordinary festival] - Miya Onsen * [http://www.aichi-kanko.jp/search/detail.asp?id=1543 Miya festival] - Aichi Prefecture Tourism Guide * {{Youtube|kO9SSWMHxTo|The Extraordinary Miya festival - Gamagori City}} - [[This is Mikawa Broadcasting Station]] * {{Facebook|38fes|Miya festival}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} ]]]] {{translated page|ja|八劔神社 (蒲郡市)|version=99684594|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Gamagori City]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1]] [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[sn:Yatsurugi Shrine]][[sn:Yatsurugi Shrine]]}} [[simple:Yatsurugi Shrine]] [[sn:Yatsurugi Shrine]] [[ja:八劔神社 (蒲郡市)]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Gamagōri]] [[Category:History of Gamagori]] {{Commons category|Yatsurugi-jinja (Gamagori, Aichi)}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image=Gamagori city Yatsurugi-jinja Shaden.jpg|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Yatsurugi Shrine | image=[[File:Gamagori city Yatsurugi-jinja Shaden.jpg|250px]]
Worship Hall | location=[[Aichi Prefecture]], [[Gamagori City]], [[Miyacho]] Nanaho 139 | coordinates={{Coord|34|48|59.4|N|137|14|53.0|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Yatsurugi Shrine (Gamagori City)}} | deity=[[Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru no Mikoto]] | type=Former [[Gōsha]] | established=[[Kanji (era)|Kanji]] 3 (1089) | 札所等= | festival=3rd and 4th [[Saturday]] and [[Sunday]] of [[October]] }} [[File:Miyamatsuri.JPG|link=|thumb|220x220px|Underwater [[shinko-shiki]]]]Yatsurugi-jinja (八劔神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] famous for its underwater [[Shinko-shiki]]. It has floating {{ill|mikoshi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mikoshi|qq=}} that are carried by people across the water in a parade.{{Cite web|url=https://morethanrelo.com/en/floating-mikoshi-miya-festival/|access-date=2023-04-12|website=morethanrelo.com}} {{出典の明記|date=2020th year 4月}} {{統合文字|劔}} {{location map|Japan mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|34.8165|137.248056}}|caption=Yatsurugi Shrine|width=256}} '''Yatsurugi Shrine''' (やつるぎ じんじゃ, Yatsurugi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in Miyacho, [[Gamagori City]], [[Aichi Prefecture]]. The shrine is classified as a former [[Gōsha]] and is known for hosting the {{ill|Miya Matsuri|ja|三谷祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miya Matsuri|qq=}}. == History == According to the [[Kuni no Myojin Meisho Chō]], the shrine is listed as "Gojōi no Kami, Yatsurugi Tenjin, located in Hōge District." The shrine is an ancient shrine that holds a deep history of enshrining a spirit originally residing in [[Atsuta Shrine]] in [[:en:Aichi District|Aichi District]], [[Owari Province]]. Initially, Yatsurugi Shrine was the guardian deity of the Miyacho area, worshipped by the locals and revered as a war deity by successive warriors. The shrine is said to have been established in [[:en:1089|1089]] (Kanji 3). It fell into disrepair at one point, but in [[:en:1190|1190]] (Kenkyū 1), on the orders of [[Minamoto no Yoritomo]], [[Adachi Tōkurō Morinaga]] oversaw the reconstruction of the shrine buildings. [[Tokugawa Ieyasu]] visited the shrine to pray for success in battle during his travels to [[Suruga Province]] in [[:en:1548|1548]] (Tenbun 16) and again in [[:en:1582|1582]] (Tenshō 10) on his return to [[Hamamatsu]]. In [[:en:1600|1600]] (Keichō 5), the local lord donated shrine lands. Subsequent lords continued this practice, which continued until the [[Meiji Restoration]]. The records in "Mikawa Futamatsukigami" state that Yatsurugi Daimyōjin Shrine in Miyacho held six koku, six to, four shō, and six gō of land. In [[:en:1885|1885]] (Meiji 17), the shrine was officially designated as a [[Gōsha]]. The shrine grounds once covered an area of four chō and four faces, but were reduced during the [[Sengoku period]]. However, the current precincts still cover over 1800 tsubo, with old pine trees surrounding the shrine buildings, evoking a sense of ancient times. The shrine grounds include several subsidiary shrines: Genta Shrine, Tenman Shrine, Suwa Shrine, Inari Shrine, Mikwa Shrine, Akiba Shrine, Shakuchi Shrine, Suijin Shrine, and Konpira Shrine. == Miya festival == {{See also|Miya festival}} [[File:Miyamatsuri.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Sea procession of the {{ill|Miya Matsuri|ja|三谷祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miya Matsuri|qq=}}]] The {{ill|Miya Matsuri|ja|三谷祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miya Matsuri|qq=}} is a famous local event known for its grand procession of splendid floats and traditional performances. The festival, celebrated at both Yatsurugi Shrine and Wakamiya Shrine in Miyacho, is said to have started in [[:en:1696|1696]] (Genroku 9) when the village headman Sazaemon received a divine revelation in a dream to conduct a mikoshi procession. The festival, which involves the entire population of Miyacho, has preserved its ancient rituals to the present day. Highlights include the raising of flags, a grand float parade modeled after the [[Gion festival]] in Kyoto, and various dedication dances from each district. The scale and disciplined organization of the festival make it a unique event. The traditional sea procession of the floats was discontinued in [[:en:1961|1961]] (Shōwa 36) due to land reclamation but was revived in [[:en:1995|1995]] (Heisei 8) to commemorate the 300th anniversary of the festival's inception. == annual Events == * New Year's festival - [[January 1]] * National Foundation festival - [[February 11]] * Prayer festival - 1st [[Sunday]] of [[February]] * festival - 3rd and 4th [[Saturday]] and [[Sunday]] of [[October]] * Shichi-Go-San (Children's festival) - Mid-October to [[November 30]] * Great Purification - [[December 31]] * New Year's Eve festival - December 31 == Subordinate Shrines == === Subsidiary Shrine === * Genta Shrine - deity: [[Otoyo no Mikoto]] === Subordinate Shrines === * Tenman Shrine - deity: [[Sugawara no Michizane]] * Suwa Shrine - deity: [[Takeminakata no Ōkami]] * Inari Shrine - deity: [[Ukanomitama no Ōkami]] * Akiba Shrine - deity: [[Kagu-tsuchi|Hi no Kagutsuchi no Ōkami]] * Mikwa Shrine - deity: [[Toyouke Ōmikami]] * Shakuchi Shrine - deity: [[Saruhiko Ōkami]] * Konpira Shrine - deity: [[Ōmononushi no Ōkami]] * Suijin Shrine - deity: [[Mizuhanome no Ōkami]] == Precincts == File:Le Temple Shintô Yatsurugi-jinja - Le torii d'entrée.jpg|Stone torii gate at the entrance File:Le Temple Shintô Yatsurugi-jinja - Le temizuya.jpg|Chōzuya (purification fountain) File:Le Temple Shintô Yatsurugi-jinja - Le Temple Shintô Ten'man-sha.jpg|Tenman Shrine (subordinate shrine) File:Le Temple Shintô Yatsurugi-jinja - Le Temple Shintô Suijin-gû.jpg|Suijin Shrine (subordinate shrine) == Access == * About a 10-minute walk from [[Mikawa Mitani Station]] on the [[Tōkaidō Main Line]] ([[Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]]). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Yatsurugi-jinja (Gamagori, Aichi)}} * [http://yaturugijinjya.com/ Yatsurugi Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.38fes.jp/ Miya festival Official Website] * [https://www.city.gamagori.lg.jp/unit/kankoshoko/miyamaturi.html Miya festival] - Official website of Gamagori City * [http://www.gamagori.jp/look/detail_56_1_10_0_0.html Miya festival] - Gamagori Tourism Association * [http://www.miyaspa.com/miyamatsuri/ Miya festival - The Extraordinary festival] - Miya Onsen * [http://www.aichi-kanko.jp/search/detail.asp?id=1543 Miya festival] - Aichi Prefecture Tourism Guide * {{Youtube|kO9SSWMHxTo|The Extraordinary Miya festival - Gamagori City}} - [[This is Mikawa Broadcasting Station]] * {{Facebook|38fes|Miya festival}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} ]]]] {{translated page|ja|八劔神社 (蒲郡市)|version=99684594|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1]] [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[sn:Yatsurugi Shrine]][[sn:Yatsurugi Shrine]]}} [[simple:Yatsurugi Shrine]] [[sn:Yatsurugi Shrine]] [[ja:八劔神社 (蒲郡市)]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Gamagōri]] [[Category:History of Gamagori]] {{Commons category|Yatsurugi-jinja (Gamagori, Aichi)}}
